Press Release article 5 of 5, Source: Company Website, 237 words

October 02 2025: Events Calendar: Pinnacle West Sets Date for 2025 Third-Quarter Financial Results, Webcast/Conference Call

PHOENIX- Pinnacle West Capital Corp. (NYSE: PNW) announced today that it plans to release its 2025 third-quarter financial results before the U.S. financial markets open on Monday, Nov. 3, 2025.

That same day at 11 a.m. ET (9 a.m. Arizona time), management will host a live webcast and conference call to discuss financial results and recent developments.

To access the live session:

Join the webcast at www.pinnaclewest.com/presentations for audio of the call and slides; or

Dial (888) 506-0062 or (973) 528-0011 for international callers and enter participant access code 502381. To access the replay:

Visit www.pinnaclewest.com/presentations within 30 days for the webcast recording; or

An audio recording will be available by phone until 11:59 p.m. ET, Monday, Nov. 10, 2025, by calling (877) 481-4010 in the U.S. and Canada or (919) 882-2331 internationally and entering replay passcode 53038.

Pinnacle West Capital Corp., an energy holding company based in Phoenix, has consolidated assets of more than $29 billion, about 6,500 megawatts of generating capacity and approximately 6,400 employees in Arizona and New Mexico. Through its principal subsidiary, Arizona Public Service, the company provides retail electricity service to about 1.4 million Arizona homes and businesses. For more information about Pinnacle West, visit the company's website at pinnaclewest.com.

SECTION 4 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews

May 12 2026: Pinnacle West Capital's Energy Transition: A Strategic Overview

Pinnacle West Capital Corporation, Arizona's largest electricity provider, faces evolving grid demands as the state accelerates renewable energy adoption and manages rate pressures in a competitive market. As of 12th May 2026, Pinnacle West Capital operates through its subsidiary Arizona Public Service, which serves approximately 1.3 million customers across central and southern Arizona.

The company functions as a holding entity for Arizona Public Service Company, one of the largest vertically integrated electric utilities in the United States. Pinnacle West Capital generates, transmits, and distributes electricity over a service territory that spans roughly 14,400 square miles. Revenue primarily comes from regulated rates, which are approved by the Arizona Corporation Commission, ensuring a stable revenue stream while necessitating ongoing rate case filings to accommodate inflation and infrastructure needs.

Pinnacle West Capital's revenue model is heavily reliant on electricity sales to residential and commercial customers, with industrial and agricultural segments contributing additional diversity. The company's generation portfolio includes the Palo Verde Nuclear Generating Station, one of the largest nuclear facilities in the U.S., which remains a key asset for baseload power. Meanwhile, increasing investments in renewable energy, including solar and wind projects, are gaining traction in response to changing policy and customer preferences in Arizona.

The utility's capital expenditure strategy focuses on infrastructure modernization and grid reliability, with a strong emphasis on transmission and distribution system upgrades. Pinnacle West Capital has been proactive in pursuing rate recovery mechanisms to support investments in smart grid technology and renewable energy integration. The demographic growth in Arizona's metropolitan areas underpins a long-term demand for electricity, allowing the company to achieve revenue growth independent of rate increases.

For U.S. investors, Pinnacle West Capital provides exposure to the regulated utility sector, typically characterized by lower volatility and attractive dividend yields. The company's strategic positioning within a rapidly growing Arizona market, combined with its commitment to renewable energy, aligns with evolving investor interests in environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors. As Pinnacle West Capital continues to adapt to changes in energy demand, its focus on sustainable growth and reliable service will remain crucial for delivering value to shareholders.

value.Title: Rio Tinto Group (L:RIO) and BHP Group Limited (L:BHP) Secure Land Control for Resolution Copper Project

urn:contentItem:6JHD-4CF3-RY7C-055W-00000-00Rio Tinto Group (L:RIO) and BHP Group Limited (L:BHP) Secure Land Control for Resolution Copper Project2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSRio Tinto Group (L:RIO) and BHP Group Limited (L:BHP) Secure Land Control for Resolution Copper Project

BRITISH DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED KINGDOM EDITION

05 May 2026 15:30 BST

Rio Tinto Group (L:RIO) and BHP Group Limited (L:BHP) completed a land swap that grants their Resolution Copper joint venture control over key acreage in Arizona. The transaction was finalized on 4 May 2026 and enables a broader drilling program for a deposit estimated to contain about 40 billion pounds of copper. The joint venture holds a 55% stake in the project, with BHP owning the remaining 45%. Under the exchange, over 5,400 acres of environmentally and culturally sensitive land were transferred to the U.S. Forest Service and Bureau of Land Management. In return, the venture acquired more than 2,400 acres adjacent to the historic Magma Copper mine, creating a contiguous footprint to support exploration and development. The Ninth Circuit upheld the exchange and dismissed challenges from local plaintiffs, including the San Carlos Apache people. The joint venture plans a drilling campaign of about $500 million over the next two years, focusing on surface exploration to refine resource data and help manage costs related to the land swap. The Resolution Copper project has long been tied to U.S. efforts to diversify mineral sources and address environmental and Indigenous concerns, and the completed land swap could enable a clearer path forward for copper supply in a market shaped by demand from renewable energy and electric vehicles

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 BHP GROUP ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 BHP Group Activities

Activities

BHP Group Limited is a global resources company headquartered in Australia, with a primary focus on the exploration, extraction, and processing of essential commodities such as iron ore, copper, coal, and petroleum. Operating across multiple continents including Australia, the Americas, and Asia, the company leverages its diversified portfolio to supply materials critical to infrastructure, energy, and manufacturing sectors. Its core activities emphasize efficient mining operations, resource development, and logistics to meet global demand. Among its fastest-growing segments are copper production, fueled by the rising needs of renewable energy technologies and electric vehicles, and potash initiatives aimed at supporting agricultural expansion. BHP maintains a strong commitment to operational excellence, safety, and environmental stewardship, positioning it as a key player in the sustainable resources market. It is the largest Mining company by market capitalisation in the United Kingdom.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Pinnacle West Capital Corporation, a Phoenix, Arizona-based holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, primarily operates through its subsidiary Arizona Public Service Company (APS), which generates, transmits, and distributes electricity to millions of customers in Arizona. The company's core business focuses on providing reliable energy solutions, including a mix of coal, nuclear, natural gas, and renewable sources, serving residential, commercial, and industrial sectors. In recent years, the fastest-growing segments have been renewable energy initiatives, particularly solar power, driven by increasing demand for sustainable options, regulatory incentives, and technological advancements. This strategic emphasis on clean energy has bolstered Pinnacle West's portfolio, enhancing operational efficiency and long-term growth potential in the competitive utility market, while maintaining a commitment to environmental stewardship and grid reliability. It is the NYSE's 14th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

November 03 2025: Pinnacle West Reports 2025 Third-Quarter Financial Results

Customer growth, summer heat drive increase in sales and usage

Operating performance, customer reliability remain strong

Company expands support for community and utility bill assistance programs

PHOENIX-- Pinnacle West Capital Corp. (NYSE: PNW) today reported consolidated net income attributable to common shareholders for the 2025 third quarter of $413.2 million, or $3.39 per diluted share of common stock. This result compares with consolidated net income of $395.0 million, or $3.37 per share of common stock, for the same period a year ago. However, through the first three quarters of the current year, the company's earnings were 2.4% lower than in the first nine months of 2024.

The 2025 third-quarter results reflect an increase of about $18 million compared to the 2024 third quarter, primarily as a result of increased customer usage, customer growth and related pricing; higher transmission service revenues; lower operations and maintenance expenses; higher AFUDC; lower depreciation and amortization expenses mostly due to operations ceasing at Cholla, partially offset by increased plant additions and intangible assets. These positive factors were partially offset by the unfavorable impacts of weather effects versus the same period a year ago; higher interest charges; lower pension and other post-retirement non-service credits; higher income taxes due to higher pre-tax income and lower tax credits.

"Driven by one of the fastest-growing service territories in the country and the third-hottest Arizona summer on record, we experienced an increase in retail sales that helped contribute to solid third-quarter financial results," said Pinnacle West Chairman, President and Chief Executive Officer Ted Geisler. "In addition, our employees continued to do what they do best: maintaining reliable electric service for our 1.4 million customers.

PHOENIX- Pinnacle West Capital Corp. (NYSE: PNW) announced today that it plans to release its 2025 third-quarter financial results before the U.S. financial markets open on Monday, Nov. 3, 2025.

May 05 2026: Pinnacle West Capital Reports Q1 Revenue Surge, Outpacing Expectations

Pinnacle West Capital Corporation has announced a robust first-quarter revenue of $1.15 billion, surpassing analysts' expectations of $1.08 billion. On 6th May 2026, the company revealed that this $38 million increase was primarily driven by enhanced transmission revenues, reduced operations and maintenance costs, and a surge in customer growth and energy consumption, influenced by weather conditions in Arizona.

The positive revenue trend reflects heightened energy demand attributed to unusually high temperatures in Phoenix and surrounding areas. Pinnacle West's management noted that these weather patterns significantly boosted energy consumption among retail customers, contributing to the revenue exceeding market forecasts. Despite this growth, the company is grappling with rising interest expenses and elevated tax obligations, which somewhat tempered the overall financial gains.

Pinnacle West Capital operates primarily through its subsidiary, Arizona Public Service (APS), which serves approximately 1.4 million customers across a vast territory of 35,000 square miles in central Arizona. With a market capitalization of $12.53 billion, the company focuses on regulated electric services and holds or leases 6.5 gigawatts of power generation capacity, a substantial portion of which comes from clean energy sources.

Currently, Pinnacle West's price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio stands at 20.46, nearing its two-year high of 20.56, suggesting that the stock may be trading at a premium relative to historical valuations. The company's GF Score™ of 81 out of 100 indicates strong potential for long-term returns based on various financial metrics, although its financial strength rating of 3 out of 10 raises concerns regarding its debt levels.

Over the past year, Pinnacle West has experienced two insider sell transactions totaling $741,213, with no insider buys reported, indicating a potentially cautious outlook among company insiders. While the strong revenue growth in the first quarter is notable, the company's financial health and insider selling activity may warrant caution for potential investors considering the stock.

This strategic pivot may provide a cushion against adverse market conditions, but analysts remain skeptical about the immediate impact on financial performance.

Mon May 4, 2026: Rio Tinto and BHP Joint Venture Secures Resolution Copper Land Control

In a significant development for the mining sector, the joint venture between Rio Tinto and BHP has successfully completed a land swap that grants them control over crucial acreage for the Resolution Copper project. This transaction, finalized on the 4th of May 2026, allows the joint venture to unlock the full potential of the Resolution Copper mine in Arizona, which is estimated to hold approximately 40 billion pounds of copper.

The land exchange involves the transfer of over 5,400 acres of environmentally and culturally sensitive land to the U.S. Forest Service and Bureau of Land Management. In return, the joint venture has acquired more than 2,400 acres adjacent to the historic Magma Copper mine. The successful conclusion of this land swap follows a ruling by the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit, which upheld the exchange and dismissed legal challenges from local plaintiffs, including the San Carlos Apache people.

Rio Tinto holds a 55% stake in the Resolution Copper project, while BHP owns the remaining 45%. The joint venture's control over this acreage is expected to facilitate a significant drilling campaign estimated at around $500 million over the next two years. This drilling will focus on surface exploration to gather further resource information and manage costs associated with the land exchange.

The Resolution Copper project has been a focal point for both the U.S. government's efforts to reduce reliance on foreign mineral supplies and local Indigenous groups' concerns regarding environmental and cultural preservation. The completion of this land swap marks a pivotal moment, potentially easing tensions while advancing the United States' strategy for securing critical minerals.

As the joint venture moves forward with its plans, the implications for investors are noteworthy. With the increasing demand for copper in various industries, including renewable energy and electric vehicles, the successful development of the Resolution Copper mine could position Rio Tinto and BHP as key players in the copper market.

Wed Apr 29, 2026: BHP Group Enters Renminbi Pricing Era for Iron Ore Contracts

China has successfully negotiated a deal with BHP Group Limited to price a greater portion of its iron ore contracts in renminbi, marking a significant development in the global commodities market. This agreement, finalized following prolonged negotiations, was confirmed on the 29th of April 2026, and signals China's ambition to reshape how it pays for essential resources.

The deal solidifies BHP's role as a key supplier of iron ore to Chinese steel mills, represented by state-owned China Mineral Resources Group (CMRG), extending the partnership until June 2027. Sources indicate that the revised contracts will now incorporate domestic benchmarks priced in renminbi, thereby diminishing the reliance on traditional US dollar-based pricing indices that have dominated the $190 billion iron ore market.

This outcome reflects a broader trend where global miners, including BHP Group, are increasingly compelled to adapt to the preferences of their largest customer, China. The new pricing structure introduces a more complex deal arrangement, featuring multiple benchmarks, including renminbi-denominated indices that align with iron ore prices at Chinese ports. Such changes are aimed at providing more predictable costs for steel producers in China while enhancing local economic conditions' influence on pricing.

BHP's agreement with CMRG also includes provisions for larger discounts on imported iron ore, highlighting the shifting dynamics in the negotiation power between Chinese buyers and global suppliers. While BHP has not confirmed specific details regarding the pricing mechanisms, the implications for the industry are clear, as competitors like Rio Tinto and Fortescue Metals Group are also in discussions with CMRG regarding similar adjustments.

As the global market adapts to these changes, BHP Group's move to incorporate renminbi pricing into long-term contracts could pave the way for a fundamental shift in iron ore pricing strategies and further cement China's position in international trade. This development is likely to influence negotiations and pricing models across the mining sector, as companies seek to align with China's evolving economic landscape.

Tue Apr 28, 2026: BHP Group Limited's New CEO Emphasizes Growth and Strategic Opportunities

BHP Group Limited has appointed Brandon Craig as its new chief executive officer, succeeding Mike Henry effective July 1, 2026. This strategic move comes as BHP continues to strengthen its leadership in the global mining sector. Craig's extensive experience within the company, spanning over 25 years, positions him well to lead BHP into its next phase of growth.

On 28th April 2026, BHP's Chair Ross McEwan expressed confidence in Craig's capabilities, highlighting his past performance and strategic vision for the company. Under Henry's leadership, BHP improved its safety protocols, productivity, and financial standing, setting a solid foundation for Craig to build upon. McEwan noted that Craig's focus on discipline and high-performance culture aligns well with BHP's ongoing commitment to maximizing shareholder returns.

Craig has held various roles within BHP, most recently serving as President Americas, where he spearheaded growth strategies across key markets including the U.S., Canada, and South America. His leadership contributed to BHP solidifying its position as the world's largest copper producer and advancing major projects in both copper and potash. This experience will be instrumental as he seeks to explore new growth avenues for BHP.

In his new role, Craig has indicated a preference for growth and is open to pursuing 'incredibly compelling' deals that could enhance BHP's portfolio. This openness to strategic acquisitions reflects a forward-looking approach, aiming to capitalize on market opportunities while continuing to strengthen BHP's core operations. Investors may be particularly interested in how Craig's vision will unfold in an evolving market landscape.

As BHP Group Limited prepares for this leadership transition, all eyes will be on Craig's strategies for growth and how he plans to navigate potential acquisitions. With a focus on innovation and expansion, the new CEO's decisions will not only shape the company's future but also influence the broader mining industry.

urn:contentItem:6JGB-3VX3-RV59-41RR-00000-00Mitsui & Co. Secures $850M Cobalt Offtake in Arizona2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisMitsui & Co. Secures $850M Cobalt Offtake in Arizona

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

01 May 2026 09:47 EDT

In a significant development for Arizona's far southwest corner, Mitsui & Co. has finalized an $850 million offtake agreement to purchase the majority of cobalt metal from a planned solar-powered processing plant in Yuma County. This deal, disclosed on 29th April 2026, positions the project as a cornerstone of America’s battery supply chain, enhancing domestic capabilities in a critical sector.

According to the Phoenix Business Journal, the binding agreement encompasses nearly all cobalt metal production from the facility, while negotiations for cobalt sulfate are still ongoing. EVelution Energy, the company behind the plant, has indicated that it will initially supply Mitsui with up to 3,000 metric tons of contained cobalt per year, with an eventual capacity of approximately 7,000 metric tons annually. Construction is slated to commence in early 2027 and conclude by the end of 2029.

The importance of this agreement cannot be overstated, as cobalt is a vital component for electric vehicle batteries, aerospace alloys, and various high-tech applications. Currently, most of the global refining capacity for cobalt resides overseas, creating vulnerabilities in the supply chain. Mitsui's offtake is aimed at securing feedstock for manufacturers and traders in allied nations, thereby reducing reliance on non-allied refining networks.

The local economic impact of the project is projected to exceed $750 million, creating around 3,300 jobs through direct, indirect, and induced avenues. EVelution president Navaid Alam emphasized the necessity of establishing robust cobalt processing infrastructure within the U.S. to bolster the resilience of the nation's industrial base. However, challenges such as water use in arid conditions and future regulatory approvals are expected to come under scrutiny as the project progresses.

With Mitsui now confirmed as a primary buyer, EVelution and Mitsui must finalize pricing and contractual details for cobalt sulfate, secure comprehensive project financing, and navigate a complex array of local and federal permits before construction can begin. Company representatives have directed inquiries to their public filings and press materials, underscoring the significance of this partnership in reshaping the U.S. cobalt landscape.

SECTION 1 MITSUI & CO. ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Mitsui & Co. Activities

Activities

Mitsui & Co. Ltd., a prominent Japanese conglomerate and one of the world's largest general trading companies, focuses on global trading, investment, and development across a diverse portfolio. Its core activities encompass the import, export, and distribution of commodities, including energy resources, metals, machinery, chemicals, and consumer goods, alongside strategic investments in infrastructure, logistics, and technology. The company leverages its extensive network in over 90 countries to facilitate international business and sustainable growth. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy, such as solar and wind projects, digital transformation services, and healthcare innovations, fueled by rising global demand for clean energy and advanced technologies. This diversification positions Mitsui as a key player in addressing environmental challenges and economic development worldwide. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's 2nd largest Distribution/Wholesale company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JJW-S553-S89F-P3R9-00000-00First Solar, Inc. (NASDAQ:FSLR) Reports Secured Entire U.S. Production For Next Three Years2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisFirst Solar, Inc. (NASDAQ:FSLR) Reports Secured Entire U.S. Production For Next Three Years

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

12 May 2026 10:16 EDT

On 10th May 2026, First Solar, Inc. (NASDAQ:FSLR) reports that it has secured its entire U.S. production for the next three years, reinforcing its role in the solar power sector amid rising electricity demand and the global shift to renewables. The company specializes in the design and manufacture of solar photovoltaic panels, modules, and systems for utility scale development projects, and is recognized as the world’s largest manufacturer of cadmium telluride thin film modules

SECTION 1 FIRST SOLAR ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 First Solar Activities

Activities

First Solar, Inc., a leading American solar technology company listed on the Nasdaq, specializes in the design, manufacture, and sale of photovoltaic solar modules using advanced thin-film cadmium telluride (CdTe) technology. The company's core activities encompass the production of high-efficiency solar panels, as well as engineering, procurement, and construction services for large-scale solar projects, alongside operations and maintenance support. In recent years, First Solar's fastest-growing segments include utility-scale solar installations and international markets, driven by increasing global demand for renewable energy solutions, particularly in regions like Asia-Pacific and Europe. This expansion is fueled by technological innovations that enhance module efficiency and reduce costs, positioning the company as a key contributor to the transition toward sustainable energy. With a strong focus on environmental responsibility, First Solar continues to scale its operations while maintaining competitive advantages in cost and performance.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Pinnacle West Capital Corporation, a Phoenix, Arizona-based holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, primarily operates through its subsidiary Arizona Public Service Company (APS), which generates, transmits, and distributes electricity to millions of customers in Arizona. The company's core business focuses on providing reliable energy solutions, including a mix of coal, nuclear, natural gas, and renewable sources, serving residential, commercial, and industrial sectors. In recent years, the fastest-growing segments have been renewable energy initiatives, particularly solar power, driven by increasing demand for sustainable options, regulatory incentives, and technological advancements. This strategic emphasis on clean energy has bolstered Pinnacle West's portfolio, enhancing operational efficiency and long-term growth potential in the competitive utility market, while maintaining a commitment to environmental stewardship and grid reliability. It is the NYSE's 14th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

November 03 2025: Pinnacle West Reports 2025 Third-Quarter Financial Results

Customer growth, summer heat drive increase in sales and usage

Operating performance, customer reliability remain strong

Company expands support for community and utility bill assistance programs

PHOENIX-- Pinnacle West Capital Corp. (NYSE: PNW) today reported consolidated net income attributable to common shareholders for the 2025 third quarter of $413.2 million, or $3.39 per diluted share of common stock. This result compares with consolidated net income of $395.0 million, or $3.37 per share of common stock, for the same period a year ago. However, through the first three quarters of the current year, the company's earnings were 2.4% lower than in the first nine months of 2024.

The 2025 third-quarter results reflect an increase of about $18 million compared to the 2024 third quarter, primarily as a result of increased customer usage, customer growth and related pricing; higher transmission service revenues; lower operations and maintenance expenses; higher AFUDC; lower depreciation and amortization expenses mostly due to operations ceasing at Cholla, partially offset by increased plant additions and intangible assets. These positive factors were partially offset by the unfavorable impacts of weather effects versus the same period a year ago; higher interest charges; lower pension and other post-retirement non-service credits; higher income taxes due to higher pre-tax income and lower tax credits.

"Driven by one of the fastest-growing service territories in the country and the third-hottest Arizona summer on record, we experienced an increase in retail sales that helped contribute to solid third-quarter financial results," said Pinnacle West Chairman, President and Chief Executive Officer Ted Geisler. "In addition, our employees continued to do what they do best: maintaining reliable electric service for our 1.4 million customers.

October 02 2025: Events Calendar: Pinnacle West Sets Date for 2025 Third-Quarter Financial Results, Webcast/Conference Call

PHOENIX- Pinnacle West Capital Corp. (NYSE: PNW) announced today that it plans to release its 2025 third-quarter financial results before the U.S. financial markets open on Monday, Nov. 3, 2025.

That same day at 11 a.m. ET (9 a.m. Arizona time), management will host a live webcast and conference call to discuss financial results and recent developments.

To access the live session:

Join the webcast at www.pinnaclewest.com/presentations for audio of the call and slides; or

Dial (888) 506-0062 or (973) 528-0011 for international callers and enter participant access code 502381. To access the replay:

Visit www.pinnaclewest.com/presentations within 30 days for the webcast recording; or

An audio recording will be available by phone until 11:59 p.m. ET, Monday, Nov. 10, 2025, by calling (877) 481-4010 in the U.S. and Canada or (919) 882-2331 internationally and entering replay passcode 53038.

Pinnacle West Capital Corp., an energy holding company based in Phoenix, has consolidated assets of more than $29 billion, about 6,500 megawatts of generating capacity and approximately 6,400 employees in Arizona and New Mexico. Through its principal subsidiary, Arizona Public Service, the company provides retail electricity service to about 1.4 million Arizona homes and businesses. For more information about Pinnacle West, visit the company's website at pinnaclewest.com.

urn:contentItem:6JK3-7J23-RTTV-W0RF-00000-00Pinnacle West Capital's Energy Transition: A Strategic Overview2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisPinnacle West Capital's Energy Transition: A Strategic Overview

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

14 May 2026 07:16 EDT

Pinnacle West Capital Corporation, Arizona's largest electricity provider, faces evolving grid demands as the state accelerates renewable energy adoption and manages rate pressures in a competitive market. As of 12th May 2026, Pinnacle West Capital operates through its subsidiary Arizona Public Service, which serves approximately 1.3 million customers across central and southern Arizona.

The company functions as a holding entity for Arizona Public Service Company, one of the largest vertically integrated electric utilities in the United States. Pinnacle West Capital generates, transmits, and distributes electricity over a service territory that spans roughly 14,400 square miles. Revenue primarily comes from regulated rates, which are approved by the Arizona Corporation Commission, ensuring a stable revenue stream while necessitating ongoing rate case filings to accommodate inflation and infrastructure needs.

Pinnacle West Capital's revenue model is heavily reliant on electricity sales to residential and commercial customers, with industrial and agricultural segments contributing additional diversity. The company's generation portfolio includes the Palo Verde Nuclear Generating Station, one of the largest nuclear facilities in the U.S., which remains a key asset for baseload power. Meanwhile, increasing investments in renewable energy, including solar and wind projects, are gaining traction in response to changing policy and customer preferences in Arizona.

The utility's capital expenditure strategy focuses on infrastructure modernization and grid reliability, with a strong emphasis on transmission and distribution system upgrades. Pinnacle West Capital has been proactive in pursuing rate recovery mechanisms to support investments in smart grid technology and renewable energy integration. The demographic growth in Arizona's metropolitan areas underpins a long-term demand for electricity, allowing the company to achieve revenue growth independent of rate increases.

For U.S. investors, Pinnacle West Capital provides exposure to the regulated utility sector, typically characterized by lower volatility and attractive dividend yields. The company's strategic positioning within a rapidly growing Arizona market, combined with its commitment to renewable energy, aligns with evolving investor interests in environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors. As Pinnacle West Capital continues to adapt to changes in energy demand, its focus on sustainable growth and reliable service will remain crucial for delivering value to shareholders.

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 PINNACLE WEST CAPITAL RECENT PRESS RELEASES

SECTION 2 PINNACLE WEST CAPITAL ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 PINNACLE WEST CAPITAL RECENT PRESS RELEASES

April 24: Pinnacle West Capital quarterly dividends steady

Pinnacle West Capital today announced a quarterly dividend of 91.0c per share for Q1/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 91.0c. The ex-dividend date is Monday, May 04, 2026 and the record date is Monday, May 04, 2026 and it is payable on Monday, June 01.

December 12, 2025: Pinnacle West Capital quarterly dividends steady

Pinnacle West Capital today announced a quarterly dividend of 91.0c per share for Q4/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 91.0c. The ex-dividend date is Monday, February 02, 2026 and the record date is Monday, February 02, 2026 and it is payable on Monday, March 02, 2026.

October 24, 2025: Pinnacle West Capital quarterly dividends up 1.7% sequentially from previous quarter

Pinnacle West Capital today announced a quarterly dividend of 91.0c per share for Q3/2025; up 1.7% sequentially from the previous quarter (Q2/2025) dividend of 89.50c. The ex-dividend date is Monday, November 03, 2025 and the record date is Monday, November 03, 2025 and it is payable on Monday, December 01.

SECTION 2 PINNACLE WEST CAPITAL ACTIVITIES

Activities

Pinnacle West Capital Corporation, a Phoenix, Arizona-based holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, primarily operates through its subsidiary Arizona Public Service Company (APS), which generates, transmits, and distributes electricity to millions of customers in Arizona. The company's core business focuses on providing reliable energy solutions, including a mix of coal, nuclear, natural gas, and renewable sources, serving residential, commercial, and industrial sectors. In recent years, the fastest-growing segments have been renewable energy initiatives, particularly solar power, driven by increasing demand for sustainable options, regulatory incentives, and technological advancements. This strategic emphasis on clean energy has bolstered Pinnacle West's portfolio, enhancing operational efficiency and long-term growth potential in the competitive utility market, while maintaining a commitment to environmental stewardship and grid reliability. It is the NYSE's 14th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Pinnacle West Capital Corporation, a Phoenix, Arizona-based holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, primarily operates through its subsidiary Arizona Public Service Company (APS), which generates, transmits, and distributes electricity to millions of customers in Arizona. The company's core business focuses on providing reliable energy solutions, including a mix of coal, nuclear, natural gas, and renewable sources, serving residential, commercial, and industrial sectors. In recent years, the fastest-growing segments have been renewable energy initiatives, particularly solar power, driven by increasing demand for sustainable options, regulatory incentives, and technological advancements. This strategic emphasis on clean energy has bolstered Pinnacle West's portfolio, enhancing operational efficiency and long-term growth potential in the competitive utility market, while maintaining a commitment to environmental stewardship and grid reliability. It is the NYSE's 14th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Brown Forman Corporation (NYSE:BF.B) is a leading force in the premium spirits industry, renowned for its robust Brown-Forman whiskey brands, including Jack Daniel's Tennessee Whiskey, Woodford Reserve Bourbon, and Old Forester Bourbon. Its diverse Brown-Forman spirits portfolio prominently features iconic names such as Jack Daniel's Old No. 7, Woodford Reserve Double Oaked, and Old Forester 1920 Prohibition Style. Emphasizing strategic growth, the company capitalizes on consumer demand for premium bourbon brands and limited edition whiskey releases like Jack Daniel's Single Barrel and Woodford Reserve Master's Collection. Investors remain intrigued by recent Brown-Forman acquisition news and the potential implications of Sazerac Brown-Forman talks. Market analysts closely monitor Brown-Forman M&A news and the impact of potential takeover rumors on Brown-Forman NYSE BF.B stock news. As small batch bourbon and Tennessee whiskey brands continue to capture global attention, Brown Forman's strategic positioning and expansion into Buy Jack Daniel's online platforms bolster its growth outlook.

November 03 2025: Pinnacle West Reports 2025 Third-Quarter Financial Results

Customer growth, summer heat drive increase in sales and usage

Operating performance, customer reliability remain strong

Company expands support for community and utility bill assistance programs

PHOENIX-- Pinnacle West Capital Corp. (NYSE: PNW) today reported consolidated net income attributable to common shareholders for the 2025 third quarter of $413.2 million, or $3.39 per diluted share of common stock. This result compares with consolidated net income of $395.0 million, or $3.37 per share of common stock, for the same period a year ago. However, through the first three quarters of the current year, the company's earnings were 2.4% lower than in the first nine months of 2024.

The 2025 third-quarter results reflect an increase of about $18 million compared to the 2024 third quarter, primarily as a result of increased customer usage, customer growth and related pricing; higher transmission service revenues; lower operations and maintenance expenses; higher AFUDC; lower depreciation and amortization expenses mostly due to operations ceasing at Cholla, partially offset by increased plant additions and intangible assets. These positive factors were partially offset by the unfavorable impacts of weather effects versus the same period a year ago; higher interest charges; lower pension and other post-retirement non-service credits; higher income taxes due to higher pre-tax income and lower tax credits.

"Driven by one of the fastest-growing service territories in the country and the third-hottest Arizona summer on record, we experienced an increase in retail sales that helped contribute to solid third-quarter financial results," said Pinnacle West Chairman, President and Chief Executive Officer Ted Geisler. "In addition, our employees continued to do what they do best: maintaining reliable electric service for our 1.4 million customers.

Press Release article 5 of 5, Source: Company Website, 237 words

October 02 2025: Events Calendar: Pinnacle West Sets Date for 2025 Third-Quarter Financial Results, Webcast/Conference Call

PHOENIX- Pinnacle West Capital Corp. (NYSE: PNW) announced today that it plans to release its 2025 third-quarter financial results before the U.S. financial markets open on Monday, Nov. 3, 2025.

That same day at 11 a.m. ET (9 a.m. Arizona time), management will host a live webcast and conference call to discuss financial results and recent developments.

To access the live session:

Join the webcast at www.pinnaclewest.com/presentations for audio of the call and slides; or

Dial (888) 506-0062 or (973) 528-0011 for international callers and enter participant access code 502381. To access the replay:

Visit www.pinnaclewest.com/presentations within 30 days for the webcast recording; or

An audio recording will be available by phone until 11:59 p.m. ET, Monday, Nov. 10, 2025, by calling (877) 481-4010 in the U.S. and Canada or (919) 882-2331 internationally and entering replay passcode 53038.

Pinnacle West Capital Corp., an energy holding company based in Phoenix, has consolidated assets of more than $29 billion, about 6,500 megawatts of generating capacity and approximately 6,400 employees in Arizona and New Mexico. Through its principal subsidiary, Arizona Public Service, the company provides retail electricity service to about 1.4 million Arizona homes and businesses. For more information about Pinnacle West, visit the company's website at pinnaclewest.com.

May 04 2026: Pinnacle West Capital Corporation Sees Strong Revenue Growth in Q1

Pinnacle West Capital Corporation reported a significant improvement in its financial performance for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026. On 5th May 2026, the company announced that sales reached USD 1,149.6 million, a notable increase from USD 1,032.28 million in the same quarter last year.

The company reported a net income of USD 32.92 million, contrasting sharply with a net loss of USD 4.64 million over the same period in the previous year. This turnaround reflects a robust operational strategy and market conditions that have favored Pinnacle West Capital Corporation, allowing it to regain profitability.

Basic earnings per share from continuing operations stood at USD 0.27, compared to a basic loss per share of USD 0.04 a year earlier. Similarly, diluted earnings per share from continuing operations also reported at USD 0.27, marking a significant recovery from the diluted loss per share of USD 0.04 reported in Q1 of the previous year.

Pinnacle West Capital Corporation's positive earnings outlook is further supported by its superior composite rating. This rating is derived from a weighted average of various factors, including fundamentals, valuation, and financial health, which collectively suggest a promising trajectory for the company.

Additionally, the MSCI ESG score indicates that Pinnacle West Capital Corporation is performing well in terms of environmental, social, and governance practices compared to its industry peers. Investors may find this aspect particularly appealing as they consider sustainability risks and opportunities in their investment decisions.

May 04 2026: Pinnacle West Capital Corporation Releases Q1 2026 Financial Insights

Pinnacle West Capital Corporation (NYSE: PNW) has published its Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the period ending March 31, 2026. This report provides significant updates regarding the financial and operational status of both Pinnacle West and its major subsidiary, Arizona Public Service Company (APS), offering crucial insights for investors and stakeholders.

As of April 28, 2026, Pinnacle West had 121,187,166 shares of common stock outstanding, while APS reported 71,264,947 shares. Both firms are compliant with the filing requirements under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and maintain an active status in their SEC filings. Pinnacle West is classified as a large accelerated filer, reinforcing its commitment to robust corporate governance and transparency in financial reporting.

The Q1 2026 report reveals that Pinnacle West and APS continue to operate under stable conditions, with no reported changes to the company name, address, or fiscal year. The absence of unusual activities suggests a consistent operational focus within the regulated electric utility sector in Arizona. In conclusion, the Q1 2026 report from Pinnacle West Capital Corporation and Arizona Public Service Company underscores ongoing regulatory compliance and operational stability. May 04 2026: Pinnacle West Capital Corporation Reports Strong Q1 2026 Earnings Amid Arizona's Manufacturing Boom

Pinnacle West Capital Corporation reported robust earnings for the first quarter of 2026, reflecting a strategic alignment with Arizona's burgeoning semiconductor and advanced manufacturing sectors. The company experienced a strong customer growth rate of 2.2% and a weather-normalized sales growth of 7.4%. The earnings call, held on the 4th of May, 2026, emphasized how these factors contributed to the company's financial performance amidst challenging market dynamics.

Key drivers of Pinnacle West's quarterly earnings included heightened transmission revenue and record-breaking heat in March, which effectively mitigated increased financing costs and depreciation. Management highlighted their ongoing commitment to enhancing grid resilience through machine learning and automation, particularly focusing on wildfire and weather risk mitigation. This strategic positioning is further reinforced by the company's support for TSMC's multi-fab expansion, with 4.5 gigawatts of large-load demand already committed.

Looking forward, Pinnacle West Capital Corporation maintained its annual sales growth guidance for 2026 at 4% to 6%, with optimistic long-term projections of 5% to 7% through 2030. The upcoming Integrated Resource Plan (IRP) filing will outline a 10 to 15-year framework for the company's generation and transmission needs, integrating organic growth with committed high-load factors. Management is also focused on narrowing regulatory lag to within 50 basis points of the authorized Return on Equity (ROE) by 2029, leveraging constructive rate case outcomes.

In terms of operational efficiency, Pinnacle West's cost management strategy reflected a significant decrease in O&M expenses driven by lower planned outage costs and a Commission-mandated reduction in energy efficiency programs. Additionally, the retirement of the Cholla coal plant has impacted depreciation, but management is evaluating the site for potential conversion to natural gas generation, which could offer financial advantages amidst rising supply chain pressures.

Pinnacle West Capital Corporation's innovative subscription model for large-load customers is set to facilitate filed agreements this year, potentially converting some of the 20-gigawatt uncommitted queue into committed capacity. While the company is supportive of federal permitting reform for transmission and pipelines, it remains focused on executing its current infrastructure plan without relying on external reforms. With a capital allocation strategy prioritizing de-risking its equity plan, Pinnacle West is well-positioned for future growth amidst the evolving energy landscape.

urn:contentItem:6JHK-Y103-S8YC-S337-00000-00Rio Tinto and BHP Joint Venture Gains Control of Resolution Copper Acreage2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisRio Tinto and BHP Joint Venture Gains Control of Resolution Copper Acreage

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

06 May 2026 10:00 EDT

Rio Tinto and BHP Group Limited have made significant strides in their joint venture with the completion of a crucial land swap, securing control over the Resolution Copper project acreage in Arizona. This development comes as a response to the ongoing need for domestic copper resources, particularly as the U.S. aims to reduce reliance on foreign supplies. The announcement was made public on the 4th of May 2026, following a favorable ruling from the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit.

The land exchange involves the transfer of more than 5,400 acres of culturally and environmentally sensitive land that contains Native American cultural sites to the U.S. Forest Service and Bureau of Land Management. In return, the joint venture has received over 2,400 acres adjacent to the historic Magma Copper mine, which is estimated to contain approximately 40 billion pounds of copper. This strategic move is expected to unlock the full potential of the Resolution Copper project, which is crucial for both companies and the broader mining sector.

In light of the land acquisition, the Resolution Copper project plans to initiate a drilling campaign estimated at $500 million over the next two years. This campaign will focus on surface drilling to gather additional resource information and manage closeout and mitigation costs associated with the land exchange. Such investments signal the commitment of both Rio Tinto and BHP to advance this significant mining venture, aiming to contribute substantially to the U.S. copper supply chain.

The completion of this land swap marks a pivotal moment in a long-standing legal battle involving the San Carlos Apache people and the U.S. government's efforts to secure critical mineral resources. With the legal hurdles now cleared, the joint venture can proceed with its plans to develop the Resolution Copper mine, a project that has faced numerous delays and challenges in recent years. The outcome is seen as a positive development for investors looking to capitalize on the growing demand for copper, driven by the energy transition and technological advancements.

As Rio Tinto and BHP move forward with their joint venture, the implications for the copper market and overall mining sector remain significant. The Resolution Copper project is poised to become a key player in meeting the increasing demand for copper, particularly in renewable energy technologies and electric vehicles. Investors will be closely watching how this partnership unfolds, as it could set the tone for future mining endeavors and collaborations in the region.

SECTION 1 BHP ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 BHP Activities

Activities

BHP Group Limited is a leading global resources company headquartered in Melbourne, Australia, specializing in the exploration, development, production, and marketing of essential commodities. Its core business activities span mining and processing of iron ore, copper, coal, petroleum, and other minerals, with major operations in Australia, the Americas, and beyond. The company plays a vital role in supplying raw materials for steelmaking, energy production, and infrastructure, while prioritizing sustainable practices and technological innovation. In recent years, BHP's fastest-growing segments have been copper production, driven by surging demand from renewable energy technologies and electric vehicles, and potash development for agricultural applications. These areas benefit from strategic investments in high-potential assets, such as the Olympic Dam and Jansen projects, positioning BHP for robust expansion amid global decarbonization efforts and resource security needs. Through efficient operations and a focus on safety, BHP continues to deliver value to shareholders and contribute to economic development worldwide. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's largest Mining company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JHC-5083-RRTS-00SF-00000-00Rio Tinto Group (L:RIO) and BHP Group Limited (L:BHP) Move Forward With $500 Million Drilling Plan at Resolution Copper2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSRio Tinto Group (L:RIO) and BHP Group Limited (L:BHP) Move Forward With $500 Million Drilling Plan at Resolution Copper

BRITISH DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED KINGDOM EDITION

06 May 2026 09:30 BST

Rio Tinto Group (L:RIO) and BHP Group Limited (L:BHP) finalize a land swap that gives their joint venture control of essential acreage for the Resolution Copper project in Arizona. The completion occurred on 4 May 2026, enabling a drilling campaign valued at about $500 million over the next two years to advance surface exploration and resource assessment. The project is 55% owned by Rio Tinto and 45% by BHP. The exchange shifts more than 5,400 acres of land to the U.S. Forest Service and Bureau of Land Management in exchange for over 2,400 acres adjacent to the Magma Copper mine, creating a contiguous footprint for development. The Ninth Circuit upheld the exchange and dismissed challenges from local plaintiffs, including the San Carlos Apache. The drilling program will support resource estimation and cost management tied to the land swap, while the broader project supports U.S. mineral security and copper supply considerations as demand from renewables and EVs remains robust

SECTION 1 BHP GROUP ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 BHP Group Activities

Activities

BHP Group Limited is a global resources company headquartered in Australia, with a primary focus on the exploration, extraction, and processing of essential commodities such as iron ore, copper, coal, and petroleum. Operating across multiple continents including Australia, the Americas, and Asia, the company leverages its diversified portfolio to supply materials critical to infrastructure, energy, and manufacturing sectors. Its core activities emphasize efficient mining operations, resource development, and logistics to meet global demand. Among its fastest-growing segments are copper production, fueled by the rising needs of renewable energy technologies and electric vehicles, and potash initiatives aimed at supporting agricultural expansion. BHP maintains a strong commitment to operational excellence, safety, and environmental stewardship, positioning it as a key player in the sustainable resources market. It is the largest Mining company by market capitalisation in the United Kingdom.

urn:contentItem:6JHB-59C3-S8C5-11TH-00000-00Rio Tinto Group (L:RIO) and BHP Group Limited (L:BHP) Advance Resolution Copper Development After Court Upholds Land Transfer2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSRio Tinto Group (L:RIO) and BHP Group Limited (L:BHP) Advance Resolution Copper Development After Court Upholds Land Transfer

BRITISH DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED KINGDOM EDITION

06 May 2026 03:30 BST

Rio Tinto Group (L:RIO) and BHP Group Limited (L:BHP) completed a land swap that grants their joint venture control of key acreage for the Resolution Copper project in Arizona. The deal was finalized on 4 May 2026, enabling further exploration of an ore body estimated to contain about 40 billion pounds of copper. The joint venture holds 55% of the project, with BHP owning 45%. The exchange transfers over 5,400 acres to the U.S. Forest Service and Bureau of Land Management. In return, the venture acquires more than 2,400 acres adjacent to the Magma Copper mine, strengthening a contiguous land position to support exploration. The Ninth Circuit upheld the exchange and dismissed lawsuits from local groups, including the San Carlos Apache. A drilling campaign of approximately $500 million over the next two years will focus on surface exploration to improve resource data and manage costs related to the land swap. The project remains tied to U.S. mineral security and copper supply strategies amid growing demand from energy and technology sectors

urn:contentItem:6JH5-5NH3-RWR1-N046-00000-00Rio Tinto and BHP Joint Venture Secures Resolution Copper Land Control2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSRio Tinto and BHP Joint Venture Secures Resolution Copper Land Control

BRITISH DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED KINGDOM EDITION

05 May 2026 09:30 BST

In a significant development for the mining sector, the joint venture between Rio Tinto and BHP has successfully completed a land swap that grants them control over crucial acreage for the Resolution Copper project. This transaction, finalized on the 4th of May 2026, allows the joint venture to unlock the full potential of the Resolution Copper mine in Arizona, which is estimated to hold approximately 40 billion pounds of copper.

The land exchange involves the transfer of over 5,400 acres of environmentally and culturally sensitive land to the U.S. Forest Service and Bureau of Land Management. In return, the joint venture has acquired more than 2,400 acres adjacent to the historic Magma Copper mine. The successful conclusion of this land swap follows a ruling by the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit, which upheld the exchange and dismissed legal challenges from local plaintiffs, including the San Carlos Apache people.

Rio Tinto holds a 55% stake in the Resolution Copper project, while BHP owns the remaining 45%. The joint venture's control over this acreage is expected to facilitate a significant drilling campaign estimated at around $500 million over the next two years. This drilling will focus on surface exploration to gather further resource information and manage costs associated with the land exchange.

The Resolution Copper project has been a focal point for both the U.S. government's efforts to reduce reliance on foreign mineral supplies and local Indigenous groups' concerns regarding environmental and cultural preservation. The completion of this land swap marks a pivotal moment, potentially easing tensions while advancing the United States' strategy for securing critical minerals.

As the joint venture moves forward with its plans, the implications for investors are noteworthy. With the increasing demand for copper in various industries, including renewable energy and electric vehicles, the successful development of the Resolution Copper mine could position Rio Tinto and BHP as key players in the copper market.

APPENDIX II STOCK IDENTIFIERS

SECTION 1 CORPORATE PROFILE

1.1 Activities

Pinnacle West Capital Corporation, a Phoenix, Arizona-based holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, primarily operates through its subsidiary Arizona Public Service Company (APS), which generates, transmits, and distributes electricity to millions of customers in Arizona. The company's core business focuses on providing reliable energy solutions, including a mix of coal, nuclear, natural gas, and renewable sources, serving residential, commercial, and industrial sectors. In recent years, the fastest-growing segments have been renewable energy initiatives, particularly solar power, driven by increasing demand for sustainable options, regulatory incentives, and technological advancements. This strategic emphasis on clean energy has bolstered Pinnacle West's portfolio, enhancing operational efficiency and long-term growth potential in the competitive utility market, while maintaining a commitment to environmental stewardship and grid reliability. It is the NYSE's 14th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

BJs Wholesale Club Holdings Inc (NYSE:BJ) is a leading operator in the wholesale retail sector, showcasing significant BJ's membership growth. The company thrives on a robust BJ's loyalty program that emphasizes BJ's membership benefits, including BJ's Perks Rewards and the BJ's Inner Circle membership. These initiatives enhance BJ's membership retention and drive a robust BJ's membership renewal rate, supported by seamless BJ's membership sign-up processes. A compelling offer includes BJ's Perks 5% back and the convenience of BJ's coupons and BJ's digital coupons. With expanding BJ's online shopping, BJ's same-day delivery, and BJ's curbside pickup, customer engagement is at an all-time high. Members benefit from BJ's gas station and enjoy BJ's gas discounts. Essential brands like Wellsley Farms BJ's and Berkley Jensen BJ's, along with BJ's bulk groceries and BJ's wholesale deals, form the backbone of offerings. A strategic BJ's club locator, BJ's gift cards, and BJ's business membership options further stimulate BJ's membership and revenue growth. The harmony in BJ's membership statistics, BJ's new member promotions, and BJ's membership cancellation rate underscores BJ's loyalty impact on sales, positioning the company for sustained growth.

November 03 2025: Pinnacle West Reports 2025 Third-Quarter Financial Results

Customer growth, summer heat drive increase in sales and usage

Operating performance, customer reliability remain strong

Company expands support for community and utility bill assistance programs

PHOENIX-- Pinnacle West Capital Corp. (NYSE: PNW) today reported consolidated net income attributable to common shareholders for the 2025 third quarter of $413.2 million, or $3.39 per diluted share of common stock. This result compares with consolidated net income of $395.0 million, or $3.37 per share of common stock, for the same period a year ago. However, through the first three quarters of the current year, the company's earnings were 2.4% lower than in the first nine months of 2024.

The 2025 third-quarter results reflect an increase of about $18 million compared to the 2024 third quarter, primarily as a result of increased customer usage, customer growth and related pricing; higher transmission service revenues; lower operations and maintenance expenses; higher AFUDC; lower depreciation and amortization expenses mostly due to operations ceasing at Cholla, partially offset by increased plant additions and intangible assets. These positive factors were partially offset by the unfavorable impacts of weather effects versus the same period a year ago; higher interest charges; lower pension and other post-retirement non-service credits; higher income taxes due to higher pre-tax income and lower tax credits.

"Driven by one of the fastest-growing service territories in the country and the third-hottest Arizona summer on record, we experienced an increase in retail sales that helped contribute to solid third-quarter financial results," said Pinnacle West Chairman, President and Chief Executive Officer Ted Geisler. "In addition, our employees continued to do what they do best: maintaining reliable electric service for our 1.4 million customers.

PHOENIX- Pinnacle West Capital Corp. (NYSE: PNW) announced today that it plans to release its 2025 third-quarter financial results before the U.S. financial markets open on Monday, Nov. 3, 2025.

That same day at 11 a.m. ET (9 a.m. Arizona time), management will host a live webcast and conference call to discuss financial results and recent developments.

Pinnacle West Capital Corp., an energy holding company based in Phoenix, has consolidated assets of more than $29 billion, about 6,500 megawatts of generating capacity and approximately 6,400 employees in Arizona and New Mexico. Through its principal subsidiary, Arizona Public Service, the company provides retail electricity service to about 1.4 million Arizona homes and businesses. May 03 2026: Pinnacle West Capital Gains Momentum Amid Arizona's Growth

Pinnacle West Capital Corporation (NYSE: PNW) has seen its stock price increase by approximately 17% year to date, recently trading near $104. This sustained rise comes as Arizona experiences rapid population growth and heightened electricity demand from data centers, drawing renewed interest from investors. The stock's performance is particularly noteworthy as it approaches its 52-week high of $105, with the market keenly awaiting the release of Q1 earnings on May 4th, 2026.

The 3rd of May 2026 marks an important moment for Pinnacle West Capital, which has shown resilience and growth potential in its core regulated utility subsidiary, Arizona Public Service (APS). In February, the company reported a strong Q4 2025 earnings beat, with adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $0.13 significantly surpassing analyst expectations of $0.04. For the full year 2025, Pinnacle West Capital achieved a net income of $617 million, showcasing its ability to expand its customer base while benefiting from higher rates approved by state regulators.

A significant long-term strategy for Pinnacle West Capital involves the planned renewal of the Palo Verde nuclear license, one of the largest nuclear plants in the U.S., scheduled for 2027. This move not only extends the plant's operational life but also aligns with the growing bipartisan support for nuclear energy as a clean power source. Additionally, APS's recent $600 million notes offering will fund its expanding capital program, further solidifying its position in a rapidly evolving energy landscape.

Looking ahead, the upcoming Q1 2026 earnings report will serve as a pivotal catalyst for Pinnacle West Capital. Analysts are particularly focused on demand growth, ongoing rate case updates, and capital spending guidance. Any positive developments in these areas could bolster near-term earnings forecasts and potentially sustain the stock's upward trajectory. The company’s aggressive investments in infrastructure, alongside Arizona's robust economic growth, position Pinnacle West Capital favorably in a competitive market.

While the current valuation model estimates a target price of $120 for Pinnacle West Capital, reflecting an implied upside of 16.3% over 2.7 years, the stock's appeal lies not only in its growth potential but also in its 3.5% quarterly dividend yield. This combination of stable returns and income generation makes Pinnacle West Capital an attractive option for income-focused investors, particularly as the demand for electricity continues to rise in Arizona's expanding tech infrastructure.

May 01 2026: Pinnacle West Prepares for Earnings Report Amid Rate Case Scrutiny

Pinnacle West Capital Corporation faces a critical test Monday when it reports first-quarter earnings before the market opens, with investors seeking clarity on the Arizona utility’s pending rate case and booming demand growth even as the company navigates a seasonal loss. The date of this report is 4th May 2026.

Analysts expect the Phoenix-based utility to post a loss of 5 cents per share on revenue of $1.09 billion for the quarter ended March 31. That marks a sequential decline from the prior quarter, when Pinnacle West earned 13 cents per share on $1.13 billion in revenue. The seasonal weakness reflects lower winter electricity demand, typical for Arizona utilities.

Despite the expected loss, EPS estimates have surged over the past two months, rising 98% as analysts revised their outlook less negative. Revenue estimates, meanwhile, have remained essentially flat over the same period. The stock trades at $103.72, near its 52-week high of $104.92, suggesting investors are looking past the seasonal trough.

Wall Street maintains a neutral stance on the shares, with 18 analysts assigning a consensus Hold rating and a mean price target of $105.57—implying modest 1.8% upside. The analyst community is divided, with four Buy ratings, 13 Holds and one Sell. Recent calls from BMO Capital, Morgan Stanley and Truist Securities have all maintained Hold ratings with price targets ranging from $98 to $108.

The pending rate case dominates the earnings narrative. Arizona Public Service, Pinnacle West’s main subsidiary, is seeking a $694 million revenue increase—translating to a 14.75% customer bill impact—based on a 10.70% allowed return on equity and a $13.1 billion rate base. Management commentary on regulatory progress and the timeline for approval will be scrutinized.

May 01 2026: Pinnacle West Capital Eyes Earnings Turnaround in Q1 2026

Pinnacle West Capital Corporation is poised to report its first-quarter 2026 earnings, with analysts projecting a modest return to profitability. On 4th May 2026, the utility company is expected to post earnings of $0.01 per share, a notable improvement from the loss of $0.04 per share recorded in the same period last year. The consensus estimates from eight analysts range from a loss of $0.13 to earnings of $0.15 per share, indicating a broad spectrum of expectations amidst a recovering market.

The sentiment surrounding Pinnacle West Capital has improved significantly, with the consensus EPS estimate surging 125.0% over the past month from negative $0.04, and an even more substantial increase of 200.0% from negative $0.01 over the last 90 days. This upward trajectory suggests that analysts are increasingly optimistic about the company's ability to manage costs and navigate seasonal demand fluctuations. The shift from anticipated losses to expectations of breakeven results indicates that enhancements in operational efficiency and load growth may be taking hold.

Year-over-year comparisons further highlight a recovery, with the consensus indicating a 125.0% improvement in earnings per share against the $0.04 loss recorded in Q1 2025, when Pinnacle West Capital reported a net income of just $4.6 million on revenue of $1.03 billion. Revenue for the upcoming quarter is expected to grow by 4.9%, reflecting modest customer growth and improved usage trends in Pinnacle West's Arizona service territory. If the anticipated transition to marginal profitability is realized, it could signal a significant operational shift for the company.

Seasonal patterns heavily influence utility performance, and the first quarter is typically the weakest period for electric utilities due to mild weather reducing demand for heating and air conditioning. Pinnacle West Capital serves the Phoenix metropolitan area through its Arizona Public Service subsidiary, where extreme summer heat typically drives peak demand. Investors will be closely monitoring how effectively the company manages operating expenses during these off-peak quarters, and whether customer growth in Arizona is translating into sufficient revenue gains to offset seasonal demand declines.

Regulatory developments will also play a crucial role in Pinnacle West Capital's earnings trajectory. As a regulated utility, the company's profitability is closely tied to rate case outcomes and capital expenditure plans approved by the Arizona Corporation Commission. Insights on pending regulatory proceedings, renewable energy investments, and infrastructure upgrades will be critical for investor confidence as the company approaches the peak summer demand season. Stakeholders will be particularly interested in management's reaffirmation of full-year guidance and commentary on customer growth and usage trends during the earnings call.

Pinnacle West operates primarily through its subsidiary, Arizona Public Service, which owns or leases 6.5 gigawatts of power generation capacity, including a significant stake in the Palo Verde nuclear plant. With a market capitalization of approximately $12.54 billion, the company is a key player in the regulated utilities industry, a sector known for its stability but also for its challenges, particularly in terms of regulatory pressures and capital expenditures.

While data on Pinnacle West's GF Value™ is not available, the company's current P/E ratio stands at 20.47x, which is close to its 2-year high. urn:contentItem:6JHD-4CF3-RYDK-81BF-00000-00How PPL Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisHow PPL Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

During President Trump's second term, corporate investment in the United States has increased, with major companies in technology, manufacturing, and healthcare allocating billions to domestic expansion. The semiconductor industry has committed over $500 billion in private-sector funding, indicating a notable reshoring trend. This investment activity is backed by enhanced tax incentives from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, including 100% bonus depreciation and 35% manufacturing credits, and tariff policies aimed at supporting domestic production.

The reshoring trend affects multiple high-value sectors. Semiconductor manufacturing investments of over $500 billion across U.S. facilities are expected to create more than 150,000 new jobs, while pharmaceutical companies have allocated over $150 billion for U.S. manufacturing investments. Foreign direct investment was recorded at $102 billion in Q2 2025, representing a 137% increase from Q1. This comprehensive restructuring is influenced by both policy incentives and supply chain resilience priorities.

This report examines investment commitments from companies across the technology, manufacturing, healthcare, and energy sectors.

PPL Corporation (NYSE: PPL)

- Company Profile: PPL Corporation is a U.S.-based utility that delivers electricity to its customers, with a global employee base of approximately 12,000.

- Announced Investment: A total of $6.8 billion is slated for 2025-2026 to grow grid capacity, update transmission infrastructure, and integrate renewable energy in its Pennsylvania and Kentucky operations.

- Recipient: PPL U.S. Electric Grid Infrastructure

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Energy

Phillips 66 (NYSE: PSX)

- Company Profile: Phillips 66 is a U.S.-based energy manufacturing and logistics firm that manages refineries and pipelines, employing around 13,000 people globally.

- Announced Investment: An allocation of $1.2 billion is planned for 2025 to enhance U.S. refineries for renewable fuels production and to grow midstream infrastructure.

- Recipient: Phillips 66 U.S. Refining and Pipeline Infrastructure

- Industries Impacted: Energy, Infrastructure

- Recipient: Plexus U.S. Manufacturing Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Electronics, Manufacturing

Activities

PPL Corporation is a major utility holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, headquartered in Allentown, Pennsylvania, and serving millions of customers across the United States. Its core business activities focus on the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, as well as natural gas services, through regulated utilities in states like Kentucky, Pennsylvania, and Virginia. The company operates a diverse energy portfolio that includes thermal, hydroelectric, and nuclear power, emphasizing reliability and efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Additionally, PPL is investing in grid modernization and infrastructure upgrades to enhance resilience and support electric vehicle adoption, driving sustainable growth in a competitive energy market. This strategic focus positions PPL as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon economy. It is the NYSE's 9th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

PPL Corporation (NYSE:PPL) is a prominent electric utility focused on providing comprehensive electric distribution and power transmission services across its markets. As a leader in energy delivery, PPL Corporation offers both residential electric service and commercial electricity services, underpinning its role as a regulated utility service provider. It is committed to modernizing the grid through strategic initiatives such as grid modernization and smart grid advancements, which include implementing smart meters and enhancing outage management capabilities. PPL Corporation is actively pursuing renewable energy projects, integrating solar power and wind energy to bolster its clean energy transition. Through a robust capital investment plan, the company invests in utility infrastructure, with transmission system upgrades and substation maintenance enhancing reliability. PPL is committed to sustainable energy solutions, engaging in energy efficiency programs, carbon reduction initiatives, and advancing the electric vehicle charging infrastructure, ensuring its strategic positioning in a growing market.

urn:contentItem:6JHD-9093-SDNH-R34B-00000-00How Phillips 66 Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisHow Phillips 66 Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

During President Trump's second term, corporate investment in the United States has increased, with major companies in technology, manufacturing, and healthcare allocating billions to domestic expansion. The semiconductor industry has committed over $500 billion in private-sector funding, indicating a notable reshoring trend. This investment activity is backed by enhanced tax incentives from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, including 100% bonus depreciation and 35% manufacturing credits, and tariff policies aimed at supporting domestic production.

The reshoring trend affects multiple high-value sectors. Semiconductor manufacturing investments of over $500 billion across U.S. facilities are expected to create more than 150,000 new jobs, while pharmaceutical companies have allocated over $150 billion for U.S. manufacturing investments. Foreign direct investment was recorded at $102 billion in Q2 2025, representing a 137% increase from Q1. This comprehensive restructuring is influenced by both policy incentives and supply chain resilience priorities.

This report examines investment commitments from companies across the technology, manufacturing, healthcare, and energy sectors.

Phillips 66 (NYSE: PSX)

- Company Profile: Phillips 66 is a U.S.-based energy manufacturing and logistics firm that manages refineries and pipelines, employing around 13,000 people globally.

- Announced Investment: An allocation of $1.2 billion is planned for 2025 to enhance U.S. refineries for renewable fuels production and to grow midstream infrastructure.

- Recipient: Phillips 66 U.S. Refining and Pipeline Infrastructure

- Industries Impacted: Energy, Infrastructure

- Recipient: Plexus U.S. Manufacturing Facilities

Phillips 66 share has been a two bagger in the past year. A year ago the Phillips 66 share price was $103.11. $1,000 would have bought 9.7 Phillips 66 shares on that day. Those 9.7 Shares would be worth $1,748 at today's share price of $180.28. Dividends reinvested are worth $14. PV$1000= $1,763.

Activities

Phillips 66, a Fortune 500 energy company listed on the NYSE, is a leading player in the global energy sector, focusing on refining, marketing, and transporting petroleum products, as well as producing chemicals and renewable fuels. Its core operations encompass midstream activities like pipelines and storage, downstream refining processes, and chemicals manufacturing, serving markets in the U.S. and internationally. The company's fastest-growing segments include midstream infrastructure, which benefits from rising demand for efficient energy logistics, and renewable energy initiatives such as low-carbon fuels and hydrogen projects, driven by the global shift toward sustainability. These areas underscore Phillips 66's strategic emphasis on innovation and diversification, positioning it to capitalize on emerging opportunities while maintaining a robust presence in traditional energy markets. It is the NYSE's 5th largest Integrated oil & gas company by market capitalisation.

Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) is a diversified energy manufacturing and logistics company renowned for its expertise in Phillips 66 crude oil refining and Phillips 66 refined petroleum products. The company's extensive portfolio includes Phillips 66 gasoline, Phillips 66 diesel fuel, and Phillips 66 jet fuel, crucial for global transportation. Moreover, it enhances market offerings with Phillips 66 lubricants, Phillips 66 base oils, and Phillips 66 asphalt. The firm’s innovative strength lies in Phillips 66 petrochemical feedstocks and Phillips 66 specialty chemicals, obtaining value from complex sources like Phillips 66 naphtha and Phillips 66 natural gas liquids. Phillips 66 propane, Phillips 66 butane, and Phillips 66 petroleum coke constitute its versatile product suite. The company also boasts a formidable Phillips 66 midstream infrastructure, featuring Phillips 66 pipeline transportation and Phillips 66 terminal storage solutions. Key initiatives include the Western Gateway pipeline project and the Phillips 66 Kinder Morgan partnership, optimizing Phillips 66 pipeline throughput and enhancing Phillips 66 oil transportation logistics. With strategic Phillips 66 fuel supply contracts, and Phillips 66 export terminals, the company showcases its robust Phillips 66 tank terminals and Phillips 66 refinery capacity.
