urn:contentItem:6JHD-4CF3-RYDK-81BJ-00000-00How Pulte Group Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisHow Pulte Group Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

During President Trump's second term, corporate investment in the United States has increased, with major companies in technology, manufacturing, and healthcare allocating billions to domestic expansion. The semiconductor industry has committed over $500 billion in private-sector funding, indicating a notable reshoring trend. This investment activity is backed by enhanced tax incentives from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, including 100% bonus depreciation and 35% manufacturing credits, and tariff policies aimed at supporting domestic production.

The reshoring trend affects multiple high-value sectors. Semiconductor manufacturing investments of over $500 billion across U.S. facilities are expected to create more than 150,000 new jobs, while pharmaceutical companies have allocated over $150 billion for U.S. manufacturing investments. Foreign direct investment was recorded at $102 billion in Q2 2025, representing a 137% increase from Q1. This comprehensive restructuring is influenced by both policy incentives and supply chain resilience priorities.

This report examines investment commitments from companies across the technology, manufacturing, healthcare, and energy sectors.

Activities

PulteGroup, Inc., a leading homebuilder listed on the New York Stock Exchange, focuses on designing, building, and selling residential properties across the United States. The company's core activities include land acquisition and development, construction of single-family homes, townhouses, and active adult communities, as well as offering mortgage financing and title services through affiliated entities. This integrated approach supports efficient operations and customer satisfaction in a competitive market. In recent years, PulteGroup's fastest-growing segments have been active adult communities, catering to retirees, and entry-level housing options, driven by demographic trends such as an aging population and first-time buyers. These areas have fueled revenue growth and expanded the company's geographic footprint, positioning it as a resilient player in the housing sector. It is the NYSE's 5th largest Construction company by market capitalisation.

PulteGroup, Inc. (NYSE:PHM) leads the U.S. homebuilding sector, strategically positioned through its diverse portfolio of Pulte Homes, Del Webb active adult communities, and Pulte new homes. Offering an expansive range of Pulte home floor plans and Pulte move-in ready homes, the company appeals to a vast market segment with Pulte single-family homes, Pulte townhomes, and Pulte condominiums. Investors recognize PulteGroup for its forward-thinking approaches, such as Pulte energy-efficient homes and Pulte smart home features, which resonate well with eco-conscious buyers. With a focus on quality, Pulte home design centers provide extensive Pulte customizable home options, catering to varied client needs. The firm's investment in Pulte new construction communities and Pulte virtual tours highlights its innovative edge. Benefiting from Pulte mortgage and financing options and a range of Pulte community amenities, PHM stock stands out amidst market peers, appealing to both seasoned investors and first-time homebuyers through strategic growth and exceptional Pulte homeowner services.

urn:contentItem:6JG9-8W63-RW8H-H335-00000-00April 30, 2026: COLONIAL GROUP INC. & PEERS: INDUSTRY NEWS DIGEST2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 30, 2026: COLONIAL GROUP INC. & PEERS: INDUSTRY NEWS DIGEST

Colonial Group Inc. is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services through subsidiaries such as Colonial Oil and Enmarket. Serving the energy, retail, and transportation sectors, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. Competing with major infrastructure firms like Kinder Morgan, the company leverages its regional expertise and integrated services. It recently expanded its renewable fuel offerings to align with global sustainability trends and continues to emphasize safety, community engagement, and investments in green energy and logistics technology.

In related industry developments, Australian wealth management peer Colonial First State announced a strategic investment in Oracle's AI-powered Fusion Cloud Applications to modernize its technological framework and improve operational efficiency. Managing AU $181 billion in assets for nearly one million Australians as of March 24, 2026, the firm is consolidating its core systems into a unified platform to accelerate innovation. Jeroen Buwalda, Chief Operating Officer at Colonial First State, noted that modernizing core systems gives their people more time to focus on supporting members. The transition incorporates Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management, Enterprise Resource Planning, and Enterprise Performance Management, which Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, stated will enhance operational agility and data-driven insights.

Within the financial sector, 1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. received shareholder approval on February 11, 2026, for a merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. With over 80% of outstanding shares represented at a special meeting, the merger proposal passed with over 99% of the votes cast in favor. Rory G. Ritrievi, Chair, President, and CEO of Mid Penn Bancorp, acknowledged the tremendous level of support for the transaction, while 1st Colonial Bancorp's President and CEO, Robert White, indicated that the merger will enhance their ability to serve customers. Upon completion, Mid Penn Bancorp is projected to hold approximately $7.5 billion in assets as of March 31, 2026, extending its footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area, Southeastern Pennsylvania, and Southern New Jersey.

Meanwhile, in the energy infrastructure space, competitor Kinder Morgan, Inc. and Phillips 66 are advancing the Western Gateway Pipeline project after securing necessary long-term shipper commitments by April 20, 2026. This newly proposed refined products pipeline system is designed to supply fuel from the Midwest and Gulf Coast directly to Phoenix, Arizona, and various markets in California. The initial open season concluded on December 23, 2025, generating substantial market interest, which prompted the companies to schedule a subsequent open season for January 2026. The project will feature a new pipeline from Borger, Texas, to Phoenix, alongside the reversal of existing infrastructure, including Kinder Morgan's SFPP pipeline system between Colton and Phoenix, to facilitate east-to-west fuel transportation. The Western Gateway Pipeline is expected to commence service in mid-2029, creating a major new fuel supply route to the U.S. West Coast.

Freeport- McMoRan Inc. (NYSE:FCX) drops to one-month low

Freeport- McMoRan Inc.'s (NYSE: FCX $56.55) stock price traded at its 31-day low of $56.51. The last time the price hit that low level was on Tuesday, Mar 31, 2026. Freeport- McMoRan Inc.'s stock price fell $1.23 (2.1%) from its previous trading session to close at $56.55. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 34.0 points (0.5%) in the day, the relative price change was -2.6%. There were 11,741,381 shares worth $664 million traded today; the volume was 0.8 times the average daily trading volume of 14.9 million shares.

+ Freeport- McMoRan Inc. (FCX: $56.55) Market Cap $81 billion, Non ferrous metals sector, P/E: 37, PV$1000 (20 yrs): $2,648

Activities

Freeport-McMoRan Inc. is a leading U.S.-based mining company specializing in the exploration, development, and production of copper, gold, and molybdenum. Headquartered in Phoenix, Arizona, the company operates large-scale mines in the Americas and Indonesia, leveraging advanced technologies to extract and process these essential metals for global industries such as electronics, construction, and renewable energy. Its core business focuses on sustainable resource management, emphasizing operational efficiency and environmental stewardship to meet rising demand. The fastest-growing segments include copper production, which has expanded rapidly due to increasing needs in electric vehicles and infrastructure projects, as well as gold operations that have benefited from higher prices and new mine developments. Overall, Freeport-McMoRan's strategic investments in high-potential assets position it as a key player in the global metals market, driving long-term value through diversified revenue streams and innovation. It is the NYSE's 2nd largest Non ferrous metals sector company by market capitalisation.

Southern Copper Corporation (NYSE:SCCO) falls in 4 out of last 5 days, 5-day fall of 5.1%

Southern Copper Corporation's (NYSE: SCCO $171.18) stock price decreased 39.0c (0.2%) to close at $171.18. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 34.0 points (0.5%) in the day, the relative price change was -0.7%. The stock fell in four out of the last 5 trading days, for a 5-day fall of 5.1%. There were 908,499 shares worth $155.5 million traded today; the volume was 0.6 times the average daily trading volume of 1.5 million shares.

+ Southern Copper Corporation (SCCO: $171.18) Market Cap $138 billion, Non ferrous metals sector, P/E: 33.2, PV$1000 (20 yrs): $32,218

Activities

Southern Copper Corporation, a New York Stock Exchange-listed company, is a leading global producer of copper and other metals, with operations centered in Mexico and Peru. Its core business activities encompass the exploration, mining, smelting, and refining of copper, alongside by-products such as molybdenum, zinc, silver, and gold, which are supplied to industries including construction, electronics, and renewable energy. The company's fastest-growing segments include copper production, fueled by rising demand from electric vehicles and infrastructure development, as well as molybdenum extraction for high-strength alloys in manufacturing. By focusing on operational efficiency and strategic investments in new projects, Southern Copper maintains a competitive edge in the global mining sector, emphasizing sustainable practices to support long-term growth. It is the NYSE's largest Non ferrous metals sector company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JJW-S553-S89F-P3Y2-00000-00Arizona Regulators Grant South32 Water Certification Waiver Amid Environmental Scrutiny2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisArizona Regulators Grant South32 Water Certification Waiver Amid Environmental Scrutiny

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

13 May 2026 09:45 EDT

The Arizona Department of Environmental Quality (ADEQ) has officially approved a Clean Water Act (CWA) Section 401 Water Quality Certification waiver for South32 Hermosa, concluding a review process that has outlined federal protections for impacted waterways. This decision was made public on May 6, 2026, following a thorough examination of 22 public comments submitted between 18th February and 20th March.

An ADEQ spokesperson indicated that the agency took the submitted comments into account, stating, "After carefully reviewing, considering, and responding to all comments received during the public comment period, the Arizona Department of Environmental Quality (ADEQ) has issued the Waiver to South32 Hermosa." This waiver is a significant milestone for South32, as it allows the company to proceed with operations at its Hermosa mine, which is located near Patagonia, Arizona.

The approval comes at a time when water quality and environmental management are crucial topics in Arizona, where water scarcity remains a pressing concern. South32's Hermosa project aims to develop critical mineral resources while adhering to environmental regulations. The company has taken steps to engage with local communities and stakeholders to address environmental concerns and ensure compliance with state and federal guidelines.

The South32 Hermosa mine is expected to contribute to local economic development by creating jobs and supporting infrastructure improvements. However, the company will need to maintain transparency and continue its engagement with both regulators and the community as it moves forward with its plans. The waiver's approval also reflects a broader trend in the mining industry, where regulatory bodies are increasingly focused on balancing resource development with environmental sustainability.

As South32 embarks on the next phase of its Hermosa project, investors will be watching closely to see how the company navigates the complexities of environmental compliance and community relations. The outcome of this venture may not only impact South32's financial performance but also set precedents for similar projects in the region.

SECTION 1 SOUTH32 ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 South32 Activities

Activities

South32 Limited is a global mining and metals company headquartered in Perth, Australia, with operations across Australia, Southern Africa, and South America. The company focuses on the production and sale of a diverse portfolio of commodities, including bauxite, alumina, aluminium, manganese, metallurgical coal, silver, lead, zinc, and nickel. Its core business activities emphasize sustainable resource extraction and processing, supporting industries such as construction, energy, and technology. Among its fastest-growing segments, manganese production has surged due to rising demand in steel manufacturing and battery technologies, while aluminium operations have expanded amid the global shift toward renewable energy and electric vehicles. South32 prioritizes operational efficiency and environmental stewardship, positioning itself as a key player in the evolving metals market through strategic investments and divestitures from less sustainable assets. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's 14th largest Mining company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JHM-RY53-RVFD-P02K-00000-00Rio Tinto Group (NYSE: RIO (ADR)) and BHP Group Limited (NYSE: BHP (ADR)) Gain Control Of Resolution Copper Acreage In Arizona2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisRio Tinto Group (NYSE: RIO (ADR)) and BHP Group Limited (NYSE: BHP (ADR)) Gain Control Of Resolution Copper Acreage In Arizona

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

06 May 2026 14:30 EDT

Rio Tinto Group (NYSE: RIO (ADR)) and BHP Group Limited (NYSE: BHP (ADR)) completed a land swap that gives the joint venture control over the Resolution Copper project acreage in Arizona. The exchange transfers more than 5,400 acres of culturally and environmentally sensitive land to the U.S. Forest Service and Bureau of Land Management in return for over 2,400 acres adjacent to the historic Magma Copper mine, which is estimated to hold about 40 billion pounds of copper. The announcement was made public on the 4th of May 2026, after a Ninth Circuit ruling favored the deal.

The partners plan a drilling campaign of about $500 million over the next two years, focusing on surface drilling to supplement resource data and to cover closeout and mitigation costs tied to the land exchange. The move signals the goal of advancing Resolution Copper toward development and strengthening domestic copper supply as the United States seeks to reduce reliance on foreign sources.

With the land swap completed, the venture aims to unlock the project’s potential and could influence copper-market dynamics as demand rises for copper in renewable energy and electric vehicle technologies. Investors will watch how the program progresses and how the partners manage costs and regulatory requirements during the project’s development

SECTION 1 BHP ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 BHP Activities

Activities

BHP Group Limited is a leading global resources company headquartered in Melbourne, Australia, specializing in the exploration, development, production, and marketing of essential commodities. Its core business activities span mining and processing of iron ore, copper, coal, petroleum, and other minerals, with major operations in Australia, the Americas, and beyond. The company plays a vital role in supplying raw materials for steelmaking, energy production, and infrastructure, while prioritizing sustainable practices and technological innovation. In recent years, BHP's fastest-growing segments have been copper production, driven by surging demand from renewable energy technologies and electric vehicles, and potash development for agricultural applications. These areas benefit from strategic investments in high-potential assets, such as the Olympic Dam and Jansen projects, positioning BHP for robust expansion amid global decarbonization efforts and resource security needs. Through efficient operations and a focus on safety, BHP continues to deliver value to shareholders and contribute to economic development worldwide. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's largest Mining company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JJW-XK43-SHD0-K4MH-00000-00Freeport-McMoRan Could Benefit from Copper Export Activity as US Current Account Deficit Narrows2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisFreeport-McMoRan Could Benefit from Copper Export Activity as US Current Account Deficit Narrows

NYSE STOCK REPORT

The U.S. current account deficit narrowed to $226.4 billion in the third quarter of 2025 from $249.2 billion in the second quarter, reducing the gap to 2.9% of gross domestic product from 3.3% and undershooting market expectations of $238 billion. The improvement was driven by a turnaround in primary income from deficit to surplus as American companies increased foreign earnings, together with an expanded services surplus and a modest decrease in the goods deficit, after a first quarter when the deficit hit $450.2 billion as businesses rushed to import goods ahead of new tariffs.

Impact on Freeport-McMoRan Inc.

The primary income turnaround could benefit Freeport-McMoRan Inc., which operates large-scale mining assets in Indonesia and South America that generate reinvested earnings recorded in the direct investment income category. Copper concentrate produced at its overseas mines and exported globally contributes to industrial supplies trade flows, while the company also runs U.S. mines and smelters, with copper exports adding to the goods trade balance, and growing global demand for copper driven by electrification and AI infrastructure buildouts could support both export activity and overseas earnings, with a weaker dollar potentially enhancing the competitiveness of U.S.-sourced copper.

A year ago the Freeport- McMoRan Inc. share price was $38.88. $1,000 would have bought 25.7 Freeport- McMoRan Inc. shares on that day. Those 25.7 Shares would be worth $1,698 at today's share price of $66.03. PV$1000= $1,698.

Activities

Freeport-McMoRan Inc. is a leading U.S.-based mining company specializing in the exploration, development, and production of copper, gold, and molybdenum. Headquartered in Phoenix, Arizona, the company operates large-scale mines in the Americas and Indonesia, leveraging advanced technologies to extract and process these essential metals for global industries such as electronics, construction, and renewable energy. Its core business focuses on sustainable resource management, emphasizing operational efficiency and environmental stewardship to meet rising demand. The fastest-growing segments include copper production, which has expanded rapidly due to increasing needs in electric vehicles and infrastructure projects, as well as gold operations that have benefited from higher prices and new mine developments. Overall, Freeport-McMoRan's strategic investments in high-potential assets position it as a key player in the global metals market, driving long-term value through diversified revenue streams and innovation. It is the NYSE's largest Non ferrous metals sector company by market capitalisation.

Freeport-McMoRan Inc. (NYSE:FCX) is a leading copper mining company and major copper producer Freeport, known for its extensive operations in Freeport copper production. With distinguished assets such as the Grasberg copper mine, Indonesia copper mines Freeport, and North American copper mines Freeport, the company plays a pivotal role in the global industrial copper supply FCX. Notably, its mining activities encompass open-pit copper mining Freeport and sulfide copper processing Freeport, leveraging porphyry copper deposits to maintain its vast Freeport mineral reserves. Apart from Freeport-McMoRan copper, FCX is a significant gold producer Freeport with involvement in gold mining FCX. As a molybdenum producer Freeport, it yields essential commodities like molybdenum concentrate FCX. An emphasis on sustainable mining Freeport and ESG Freeport-McMoRan aligns with market trends. The FCX stock reflects these robust fundamentals, and mining stocks copper exposure is key for those investing in Freeport-McMoRan considering the copper price impact on FCX and FCX dividend yield, bolstered by a comprehensive Freeport production report and diligent management of the Freeport mining assets portfolio.

value.Title: Rio Tinto and BHP Group Secure Resolution Copper Land Through Strategic Swap
value.Overview: Global Round Up - ADRs and Depository Receipts, 722words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JG9-M1R3-RT73-61FX-00000-00Rio Tinto and BHP Group Secure Resolution Copper Land Through Strategic Swap2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisRio Tinto and BHP Group Secure Resolution Copper Land Through Strategic Swap

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

01 May 2026 06:15 EDT

Rio Tinto and BHP Group Limited have successfully completed a significant land swap that grants them control over crucial acreage for the Resolution Copper mine project in Arizona. The announcement, made public on the 29th of April 2026, marks a pivotal moment for the joint venture, allowing it to unlock the project's full potential while addressing environmental and cultural concerns.

The land exchange involves transferring more than 5,400 acres of sensitive land, which includes Native American cultural sites, to the U.S. Forest Service and Bureau of Land Management. In return, the joint venture has gained over 2,400 acres adjacent to the historic Magma Copper mine, an area estimated to contain approximately 40 billion pounds of copper. This strategic acquisition is expected to significantly enhance the viability of the Resolution Copper project.

The completion of the land swap follows a ruling by the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit, which favored the Resolution Copper project and the federal government, dismissing the plaintiffs' requests to halt the exchange. This decision effectively ends a prolonged legal battle involving the San Carlos Apache people, who have raised concerns over the project's impact on their cultural heritage.

In addition to securing the land, Resolution Copper plans to initiate a drilling campaign valued at roughly $500 million over the next two years. The drilling will focus on surface activities to gather further resource information and cover closeout and mitigation costs associated with the land exchange. This campaign is crucial for the project's development and is seen as a step towards enhancing domestic copper production.

The joint venture, with Rio Tinto holding a 55% stake and BHP Group possessing 45%, is positioned to play a vital role in bolstering the U.S. supply chain for critical minerals. As the country seeks to reduce reliance on foreign sources for essential materials, the successful execution of this land swap underscores the potential of the Resolution Copper project to contribute significantly to the domestic copper market.

SECTION 1 BHP ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 BHP Activities

Activities

BHP Group Limited is a leading global resources company headquartered in Melbourne, Australia, specializing in the exploration, development, production, and marketing of essential commodities. Its core business activities span mining and processing of iron ore, copper, coal, petroleum, and other minerals, with major operations in Australia, the Americas, and beyond. The company plays a vital role in supplying raw materials for steelmaking, energy production, and infrastructure, while prioritizing sustainable practices and technological innovation. In recent years, BHP's fastest-growing segments have been copper production, driven by surging demand from renewable energy technologies and electric vehicles, and potash development for agricultural applications. These areas benefit from strategic investments in high-potential assets, such as the Olympic Dam and Jansen projects, positioning BHP for robust expansion amid global decarbonization efforts and resource security needs. Through efficient operations and a focus on safety, BHP continues to deliver value to shareholders and contribute to economic development worldwide. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's largest Mining company by market capitalisation.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJP-4SW3-S6T6-F50B-00000-00NATO Backs Renewables; U.S. Objects2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisNEWSNATO Backs Renewables; U.S. Objects

Politico reports that NATO is publicly backing renewables as an integral part of the alliance’s security as the fallout from the Iran War continues to disrupt fossil...

Politico reports that NATO is publicly backing renewables as an integral part of the alliance’s security as the fallout from the Iran War continues to disrupt fossil fuel imports. A NATO Energy Security Centre of Excellence (ESCE) report from earlier this ...

Politico reports that NATO is publicly backing renewables as an integral part of the alliance’s security as the fallout from the Iran War continues to disrupt fossil fuel imports. A NATO Energy Security Centre of Excellence (ESCE) report from earlier this year underscored the importance for allied militaries of diversifying away from imported oil products, particularly in forward and remote operating bases. The war in Iran has already called into question whether allies within NATO have enough jet fuel for their fighters and other aircraft essential to maintaining high readiness and national security. Politico quotes the ESCE: “Until today, military camps have been operated with diesel generators, which are reliable and powerful, but pose considerable logistical challenges as well as environmental disadvantages… In the future, extensive electrification of camp systems and greatly increased use of renewable energy sources are anticipated.” NATO vs EU The ESCE report notes how NATO and the EU have failed to coordinate interests in preparing for a future crisis of this sort. The EU leads a more piecemeal approach to its energy security, encumbered by political divisions between its members. The takeaway: when things go south, the military alliance goes pragmatic. The political alliance goes political. Drill, baby, drill The elephant in the room? The U.S., NATO’s most powerful member. Striving for renewable energy independence angers the Trump administration. That will be the biggest hurdle for the alliance’s 31 other members. Read the original piece here: “ NATO backs renewables as solution to energy security, despite US skepticism .” Access the full NATO ESCE report here . The post appeared first on Small Wars Journal by Arizona State University .

value.Title: Algonquin Power & Utilities Posts Lower Q1 Adjusted Net Earnings YoY
value.Overview: MT Newswires Live Briefs INVESTOR Canada, 181words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHT-PTF3-RSKJ-4115-00000-00Algonquin Power & Utilities Posts Lower Q1 Adjusted Net Earnings YoY2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisAlgonquin Power & Utilities Posts Lower Q1 Adjusted Net Earnings YoY

Algonquin Power & Utilities' (AQN.TO) adjusted net earnings were slightly lower year over year, the company said Friday, but exceeded analyst estimates.

Adjusted net earnings were US$99.6 million, or $0.13 per share, down from $109.0 million, or $0.14 per share. The result beat the earnings per share consensus estimate of $0.11 as compiled by FactSet.

The company reported a 2.2% decline in net earnings in the Regulated Services Group segment at $119.4 million. Algonquin cited slightly unfavorable weather conditions and higher operating expenses, among other factors.

The Hydro Group segment also fell to $2.1 million from $16.6 million, primarily due to income tax recovery as a result of the tax basis step-up from the Hydro Group's reorganization related to the sale of Algonquin's renewable energy business, excluding hydro.

Additionally, during the first quarter, Algonquin received orders allowing for the resolution of rate cases at Empire Electric Missouri, CalPeco Electric and New England Gas and has filed a settlement agreement for the Litchfield Park Water & Sewer in Arizona.

value.Overview: Global Round Up - ADRs and Depository Receipts, 713words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-N063-SC5B-N07N-00000-00BHP Group Ltd (NYSE:BHP (ADR)) Invests in DISA Technologies Amid Copper and Potash Expansion2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisBHP Group Ltd (NYSE:BHP (ADR)) Invests in DISA Technologies Amid Copper and Potash Expansion

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

04 May 2026 14:00 EDT

Melbourne, 2nd May 2026, 06:12 (AEST) — DISA Technologies has raised $33 million in a funding round led by Galvanize, with BHP Ventures, the investment arm of BHP Group Ltd (NYSE:BHP (ADR)), also participating. The financing backs DISA's high pressure slurry ablation technology to extract minerals from ore and waste rock, a capability that could help increase yields while reducing costs and waste. BHP Group Ltd seeks to sharpen its focus on copper and potash expansion and to pursue innovative recovery solutions at existing mines and legacy waste sites. BHP Group Ltd is among backers supporting more than 20 startups developing technologies to improve safety and efficiency. Laurel Buckner, who leads BHP's New Business Ventures, says DISA's approach is designed for broad deployment, aligning with the company's strategy to develop critical minerals essential for energy and industrial supply chains.

Greyson Buckingham, DISA's CEO, describes the funding as a milestone, and Galvanize's Cliff Ryan notes the potential to unlock value from both current and legacy mining operations. The round illustrates BHP's ongoing commitment to external innovation as it pursues copper initiatives and other mineral opportunities. Across its operational outlook, BHP Group Ltd targets the higher end of its copper production guidance for 2026 at 1.9 to 2.0 million tonnes. The company also reported record output from its Western Australia Iron Ore operations and said it received roughly $4.8 billion from its silver streaming deal at Antamina. It is advancing copper initiatives, including new permits for the Escondida concentrator and drilling at Resolution Copper, a joint venture with Rio Tinto in Arizona. Yet challenges remain, including uncertainties in the Chinese steel market and rising costs for diesel and consumables. With Brandon Craig set to take over as chief executive officer on July 1, questions remain about whether small, strategic bets like the DISA funding can meaningfully lift output and cash flow in a competitive copper market.

value.Title: SRP and Nextera Announce Agreement for 3 GW of Solar in Arizona
value.Overview: Energy Monitor Worldwide, 150words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-0C53-RS65-K1HB-00000-00SRP and Nextera Announce Agreement for 3 GW of Solar in Arizona2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisSRP and Nextera Announce Agreement for 3 GW of Solar in Arizona

Salt River Project (SRP) has announced an agreement with NextEra Energy Resources to develop 3 GW of new solar capacity in Arizona (United States) (SRP press release, 30/04/2026). The agreement calls for the construction of 500 MW of solar per year from 2029 through 2034. It is part of SRP plans to add several GW of renewable energy, including solar, as well as natural gas-fired and battery energy storage (BESS) resources.

SRP, a community-based, not-for-profit public power utility, has more than 3 GW of carbon-free resources currently serving its customers, including more than 1.5 GW of solar and more than 1.6 GW of battery storage supporting its grid. The procurement serves as a component of the utility strategy to retire coal generation by 2032. By moving solar energy into later hours, SRP intends to limit reliance on natural gas plants and maintain grid reliability during heat events.

value.Title: Western states are planning power demand a day ahead. Here’s why.
value.Overview: The system is called the Extended Day-Ahead Market, or EDAM. It builds on an existing real-time electricity trading market that has operated across the West since 2014. According to supporters of the...

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-XY03-RT2N-14Y4-00000-00Western states are planning power demand a day ahead. Here’s why.2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisNEWSWestern states are planning power demand a day ahead. Here’s why.Kaleb Roedel

The system is called the Extended Day-Ahead Market, or EDAM. It builds on an existing real-time electricity trading market that has operated across the West since 2014. According to supporters of the...

The system is called the Extended Day-Ahead Market, or EDAM. It builds on an existing real-time electricity trading market that has operated across the West since 2014. According to supporters of the new system, that real-time market has already saved cust...

The system is called the Extended Day-Ahead Market, or EDAM. It builds on an existing real-time electricity trading market that has operated across the West since 2014. According to supporters of the new system, that real-time market has already saved customers in the region nearly $7 billion by allowing utilities to trade excess power in short time windows before demand spikes. The new market expands that concept by giving utilities more time to plan ahead. “When you have a day to plan, you know where the wind is going to be blowing, you know where the sun is going to be shining,” said Brian Turner with Advanced Energy United, an advocacy group focused on clean energy and grid modernization. “And you can turn up and down your other generation, the coal plants or the gas plants.” Turner said utilities can use that added lead time to better balance supply and demand throughout the region, especially during extreme weather events, when electricity use can surge. Supporters say EDAM could also help lower costs by allowing utilities to buy cheaper power from neighboring states instead of relying only on their own generation. Rocky Mountain Power — Wyoming’s largest utility — is among the first participants in the new market, with additional Western utilities expected to join in the coming years. The launch comes as utilities across the Mountain West face growing electricity demand, rising summer temperatures and increasing pressure to integrate more renewable energy sources like wind and solar into the grid. This story was produced by the Mountain West News Bureau, a collaboration between KUNR, Wyoming Public Media, Nevada Public Radio, Boise State Public Radio in Idaho, KUNC in Northern Colorado, KANW in New Mexico, ColoradoPublic Radio, KJZZ in Arizona and NPR, with additional support from affiliate newsrooms across the region. Funding for the Mountain West News Bureau is provided in part by the Corporation for Public Broadcasting and Eric and Wendy Schmidt.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-B833-RXNK-H2RY-00000-00$2bn Sun Pond Solar Project in Maricopa County, Arizona by Longroad Energy Begins Operations2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisNEWS$2bn Sun Pond Solar Project in Maricopa County, Arizona by Longroad Energy Begins OperationsJoel Mairura

Longroad Energy’s $2bn Sun Pond solar and storage project in Maricopa County, Arizona by Longroad Energy begins operations. The facility includes a 111MW-direct current (MWdc) solar component and an 85MW-alternating current (MWac)/340MW-hour storage component. It is part of the broader Longroad Sun Streams Complex which combines four projects totaling nearly 1.6GW of capacity. Furthermore, contracts via power...

Longroad Energy’s $2bn Sun Pond solar and storage project in Maricopa County, Arizona by Longroad Energy begins operations. The facility includes a 111MW-direct current (MWdc) solar component and an 85MW-alternating current (MWac)/340MW-hour storage compon...

Longroad Energy’s $2bn Sun Pond solar and storage project in Maricopa County, Arizona by Longroad Energy begins operations. The facility includes a 111MW-direct current (MWdc) solar component and an 85MW-alternating current (MWac)/340MW-hour storage component. It is part of the broader Longroad Sun Streams Complex which combines four projects totaling nearly 1.6GW of capacity. Furthermore, contracts via power purchase agreements have been secured with Ava Community Energy and San José Clean Energy for the facility’s output. Longroad Energy chief investment officer and co-founder Charles Spiliotis said: “With Sun Pond now operational, Longroad is pleased to be expanding access to renewable energy for customers in the greater Bay Area. “Sun Pond’s battery storage system also adds firm, flexible capacity – ensuring low-cost, clean power is available when the grid needs it most.” The project is expected to generate enough electricity annually to power nearly 35,000 US homes. The neighbouring state of Texas is also advancing its own major solar projects such as the 600MW Hornet solar plant in Swisher County, Texas. Since its completion in 2025, the plant generates enough electricity to power 160,000 homes in the state yearly. Scope of Implementation on the Sun Pond Solar Project in Arizona The Sun Pond solar project in Arizona includes various phases, I to III projects. It is managed by Longroad Energy in unincorporated Maricopa County, Arizona. It is involves constructing 80-acre solar energy facilities paired with battery energy storage. The implementation scope focuses on rezoning from rural-43 to industrial. It is developing photovoltaic solar arrays, and integrating with the grid near the Palo Verde Generating Station. Here is more information about the project: Location: East of S 351st Ave, North of Baseline Rd, South of S 350th Dr, West of W Fremont Rd, Arizona Facility Type: Solar energy generation with integrated battery energy storage systems (BESS). Land Use Change: Requesting a comprehensive plan amendment to change land use from rural densities to utilities. Zoning Action: Zone change with overlay from Rural-43 to IND-2 IUPD (Industrial Use Permit District). Developer: LSH Land Holdings, LLC The project, labeled under case numbers CPA2023003 and Z2023024, aims to improve energy capacity in the area near a major existing generating station. It is also expected reduce CO₂ emissions by more than 145,000t each year. This equates to removing nearly 34,000 petrol-powered vehicles from use. Construction was managed by McCarthy Building Companies, which employed more than 300 workers at its peak. Operations and also maintenance will be managed by NovaSource Power Services and Longroad Energy’s affiliate, Longroad Energy Services. Longroad Energy’s $2bn Sun Pond solar and storage project in Maricopa County, Arizona by Longroad Energy begins operations. Project Factsheet: Project Name: Sun Pond Solar and Storage Project Location: Maricopa County, Arizona, USA Estimated Investment Value: $2 Billion Project Type: Solar PV + Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) Timeline Construction Phase: Completed prior to 2026 2026: Project officially begins operations Operational Phase: Active and also supplying power under PPAs Site & Scale Solar Capacity: 111 MWdc Storage Capacity: 85 MWac / 340 MWh Part of: Longroad Sun Streams Complex (~1.6 GW total capacity) Land Area: Approx. 80 acres per phase Project Teams Developer: Longroad Energy Land Entity: LSH Land Holdings LLC EPC Contractor: McCarthy Building Companies O&M Providers: NovaSource Power Services, Longroad Energy Services Offtakers: Ava Community Energy, San José Clean Energy Infrastructure Scope Utility-scale photovoltaic solar arrays Integrated battery energy storage system (BESS) Also grid interconnection near Palo Verde Generating Station Multi-phase development (Phases I–III) Supporting infrastructure including inverters, substations, and also transmission links Strategic Objectives Expand renewable energy supply to California markets Provide firm and also dispatchable clean power Also reduce CO₂ emissions (~145,000 tonnes annually) Support grid reliability with flexible energy capacity Challenges Land rezoning and permitting (Rural-43 to industrial use) Grid integration and transmission coordination Also managing large-scale solar + storage operations Balancing supply with contracted demand obligations Current Status Project operational and delivering clean energy Also itegrated into Longroad’s broader Sun Streams portfolio Supplying electricity under long-term power purchase agreements Fully constructed with ongoing operations and also maintenance in place

value.Title: SRP and NextEra announce agreement for 3 GW of solar in Arizona (US)
value.Overview: Key Energy News by Enerdata, 160words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-B683-S0HD-2126-00000-00SRP and NextEra announce agreement for 3 GW of solar in Arizona (US)2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisSRP and NextEra announce agreement for 3 GW of solar in Arizona (US)

Salt River Project (SRP) has announced an agreement with NextEra Energy Resources to develop 3 GW of new solar capacity in Arizona (United States) (SRP press release, 30/04/2026). The agreement calls for the construction of 500 MW of solar per year from 2029 through 2034. It is part of SRP plans to add several GW of renewable energy, including solar, as well as natural gas-fired and battery energy storage (BESS) resources.

SRP, a community-based, not-for-profit public power utility, has more than 3 GW of carbon-free resources currently serving its customers, including more than 1.5 GW of solar and more than 1.6 GW of battery storage supporting its grid. The procurement serves as a component of the utility strategy to retire coal generation by 2032. By moving solar energy into later hours, SRP intends to limit reliance on natural gas plants and maintain grid reliability during heat events. 

SRP press release, International press, International press II

(TheNewswire) Vancouver, Canada TheNewswire - May 14, 2026 — Desert Mountain Energy Corp. ("DME" or the "Company") (TSX.V: DME, U.S. OTC: DMEHF, Frankfurt: QM01) announces that, on May 13, 2026, the Arizona Court of Appeals denied a petition for special ac...

(TheNewswire) Vancouver, Canada TheNewswire - May 14, 2026 — Desert Mountain Energy Corp. ("DME" or the "Company") (TSX.V: DME, U.S. OTC: DMEHF, Frankfurt: QM01) announces that, on May 13, 2026, the Arizona Court of Appeals denied a petition for special action filed by the City of Flagstaff in the City's litigation against the Company. Although the Court's decision may not resolve all claims brought by the City, the Company will continue to defend against those claims as the matter proceeds. ABOUT Desert Mountain Energy Desert Mountain Energy Corp. is a publicly traded resource company primarily focused on the exploration, development and production of helium, hydrogen and natural gas. The Company is focused on helium extraction from different raw gas sources in an environmental and economic manner, supplying elements deemed critical to the renewable energy and high technology industries. We seek safe harbor "Robert Rohlfing " Robert Rohlfing Exec Chairman & CEO For more information, contact: Don Mosher, President & Director Tel: (604) 617-5448 E-mail:Don@desertmountainenergy.com Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements This news release contains "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the United States Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995 and "forward-looking information" within the meaning of applicable Canadian securities legislation that involve a number of risks and uncertainties. Actual events or results may differ from the Company's expectations. Such forward looking statements and information herein include but are not limited to the Offering, the anticipated closing date of the Offering, the intended use of proceeds, approval of the Offering from the TSX Venture Exchange, the filing of the Offering Document. statements regarding the Company's anticipated performance in the future the planned exploration activities, receipt of positive results from drilling, the completion of further drilling and exploration work, and the timing and results of various activities. Forward-looking statements or information involve known and unknown risks, uncertainties and other factors that may cause the actual results, level of activity, performance or achievements of the Company and its operations to be materially different from those expressed or implied by such statements. Such factors include, among others, changes in national and local governments, legislation, taxation, controls, regulations and political or economic developments in Canada and the United States; financial risks due to helium prices, operating or technical difficulties in exploration and development activities; risks and hazards and the speculative nature of resource exploration and related development; risks in obtaining necessary licenses and permits, and challenges to the Company's title to properties. Forward-looking statements are based on assumptions management believes to be reasonable, including but not limited to the continued operation of the Company's exploration operations, no material adverse change in the market price of commodities, and such other assumptions and factors as set out herein. Although the Company has attempted to identify important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those contained in forward-looking statements or information, there may be other factors that cause results to be materially different from those anticipated, described, estimated, assessed or intended. There can be no assurance that any forward-looking statements or information will prove to be accurate as actual results and future events could differ materially from those anticipated in such statements or information. Accordingly, readers should not place undue reliance on forward-looking statements or information. The Company does not intend to, and nor does not assume any obligation to update such forward-looking statements or information, other than as required by applicable law. Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release. Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK7-89S3-S2RD-H3SW-00000-00China’s US$3bil tech exit an investment warning2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisNEWSChina’s US$3bil tech exit an investment warning

BEIJING: Renewable energy manufacturer Jinko Solar Co’s recent decision to sell control of its Florida facility extends a multi-billion dollar retreat from the United States by China’s clean technology firms, as they contend with an increasingly hostile policy environment and the potential loss of Biden-era incentives. China-based companies in the sector scrapped about US$2.8bil in planned...

BEIJING: Renewable energy manufacturer Jinko Solar Co’s recent decision to sell control of its Florida facility extends a multi-billion dollar retreat from the United States by China’s clean technology firms, as they contend with an increasingly hostile po...

BEIJING: Renewable energy manufacturer Jinko Solar Co’s recent decision to sell control of its Florida facility extends a multi-billion dollar retreat from the United States by China’s clean technology firms, as they contend with an increasingly hostile policy environment and the potential loss of Biden-era incentives. China-based companies in the sector scrapped about US$2.8bil in planned US manufacturing projects last year, according to Rhodium Group. As of end-March, more than half of proposed Chinese clean-tech investments in the US announced since 2022 had been cancelled, paused or delayed, according to the group’s calculations. That’s part of a broader downturn that saw a 17% decline last year in all clean technology investment in the United States, Rhodium said in a report. Producers of solar equipment, batteries and electric vehicle technology have experienced a sharp reversal since Biden-era tax credits lured Chinese companies to announce US$5.6bil of investments in 2023 alone. Since then, US President Donald Trump’s administration has rolled back incentives and, most crucially, last year’s tax bill introduced new hurdles for manufacturers with ties to so-called foreign entities of concern. “Jinko’s decision underscores the enormous challenges facing Chinese clean-tech firms operating in the United States,” said Li Shuo, director of the China Climate Hub at the Asia Society Policy Institute. The company’s move should be seen as “a chilling message to anyone that wishes to come and build factories in the United States”, he said. Shanghai-based Jinko had agreed to sell about a 75% stake in its solar panel facility in Florida to FH Capital, a private equity fund. The main purpose of selling the stake is “to optimise its overseas asset allocation, ensure its long-term strategic layout in the United States, enhance flexibility and compliance, and facilitate its long-term development”, a Jinko spokesperson said. The decision was prompted by a need to comply with “US domestic manufacturing regulations” and to “minimise operational risks”, Jinko said in a corporate filing. Jinko’s sell-down follows similar moves by China-based competitors to scale back exposure to the United States or to exit. Trina Solar Co sold a majority stake in its Texas assembly facility in 2024, and last year Corning Inc acquired a JA Solar Technology Co plant in Arizona. Shanghai-listed Ningbo Boway Alloy Material Co said on Wednesday it will sell solar manufacturing assets in the United States to India’s INOXGFL Group due to the tightening of foreign entity requirements under Trump’s tax bill. Policy changes under Trump’s One Big Beautiful Bill Act mean it has become harder, if not completely impossible, for factories controlled by Chinese companies, or heavily reliant on China-dominated supply chains, to be eligible for lucrative manufacturing tax credits. Losing access to those credits puts Chinese-owned factories at a “huge disadvantage” compared with domestic rivals, said Rob Barnett, a senior analyst at Bloomberg Intelligence. Arizona-based First Solar Inc, the largest US solar producer, for example, told investors that it expects to receive more than US$2bil in credits this year. While Treasury Department guidance on specific ownership thresholds for tax credit eligibility isn’t expected to be issued until later this year, analysts expect them to be difficult for China-linked firms to meet. “The policy environment is getting restrictive,” said Margaret Jackson, a senior associate at the Centre for Strategic and International Studies and previously a senior counsellor for policy Department of Commerce during Biden’s tenure. — Bloomberg

GuruFocus News

May 1st, 2026 ( GuruFocus.com - Delivered by Newstex )

On May 01, 2026, ON Semiconductor Corp ON is set to release its Q1 earnings report on May 4th, following market closure. Analysts expect an earnings per share (EPS) of $0.61, reflecting a year-over-year increase of 10.9%, alongside projected revenues of $1.49 billion, up 2.8% from the previous year. Historically, ON has surpassed EPS and revenue forecasts 75% of the time over the past two years. However, recent trends show 13 upward revisions and 9 downward changes in EPS estimates, with revenue estimates experiencing 26 downward adjustments.

Market Cap: $40.52 billion

GF Score: 84/100, indicating strong overall performance

Insider Activity: Sold $17.8 million in shares over the past three months

What's Behind the News?

The upcoming earnings report for ON Semiconductor is significant as it will provide insights into the company's performance amid a challenging semiconductor market. Analysts are keenly observing the company's ability to meet or exceed expectations, especially given its historical track record of outperforming EPS and revenue forecasts. The semiconductor industry is pivotal for various sectors, including automotive and industrial markets, where ON has established itself as a leading player.

ON Semiconductor Corp, headquartered in Scottsdale, Arizona, is a prominent supplier of power semiconductors and sensors, focusing on automotive and industrial applications. With a market capitalization of approximately $40.52 billion, ON is the second-largest power chipmaker globally and the largest supplier of image sensors to the automotive market. The company is strategically pivoting towards emerging applications such as electric vehicles, autonomous vehicles, and renewable energy, positioning itself for future growth.

How Is ON Valued?

Currently, GF Value data is not available for ON Semiconductor. However, the company's P/E ratio stands at an exceptionally high 303x, indicating that the stock may be significantly overvalued compared to historical norms. This high valuation could raise concerns among investors about the sustainability of future earnings growth. For more detailed information, visit the ON stock page.

What Does ON's GF Score Tell Us?

The GF Score ranks stocks from 0 to 100 based on five key aspects: Financial Strength, Profitability, Growth, Valuation, and Momentum. Stocks with higher GF Score values have been found to generate higher long-term returns (backtested 2006-2021).

ON Semiconductor's strengths lie in its robust profitability and financial strength, as indicated by its high GF Score of 84. However, the valuation aspect raises concerns, as the stock is currently deemed significantly overvalued. This combination suggests that while the company demonstrates solid operational metrics, its high valuation may pose risks for investors. For more details, visit the ON stock page.

What Are Insiders Doing with ON Stock?

In the past three months, insiders have sold $17.8 million worth of shares, with no insider buying reported during the same period. This trend may raise questions about the confidence of insiders in the company's future performance.

What This Means for Investors

The upcoming earnings report will be crucial for ON Semiconductor, especially given its historical performance and current market conditions. While the company's GF Score indicates strong operational metrics, the significantly high valuation and insider selling activity may warrant caution for potential investors.

For the complete analysis, visit the ON stock page. You can also use the GuruFocus Stock Screener to find similar opportunities.

urn:contentItem:6JK7-S0X3-RWPD-22C5-00000-00Arizona governor backs proposed natural gas pipeline2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisArizona governor backs proposed natural gas pipeline

May 14th, 2026 (The Center Square: New Mexico — Delivered by Newstex)

(The Center Square) - Gov. Katie Hobbs announced her support for a pipeline that will bring more natural gas into Arizona.

The Democratic governor said a top priority of her administration is ensuring Arizona families and businesses have 'reliable and affordable energy.'

'With existing pipelines fully subscribed, securing additional natural gas capacity is critical to supporting Arizona's long-term prosperity,' she said. 'The Desert Southwest Pipeline will significantly increase our state's energy capacity and diversify our energy resources.'

As the state continues to grow, Arizona will need to increase its energy demand, Hobbs said.

She added that the Desert Southwest Pipeline project 'ensures that during periods of high demand, we have the energy we need to meet it.'

Vicki Granado, vice president of public relations and corporate communication for Energy Transfer, said the company is pleased to have the governor's support of its pipeline, which is in the planning stage.

Energy Transfer, one of America's biggest pipeline companies, expects to begin construction in the second quarter of 2028 and complete it by the fourth quarter of 2029, Granado told The Center Square on Wednesday.

The 520-mile-long underground pipeline starts in Texas and goes through New Mexico and Arizona, according to the company's website.

Granado said the pipeline will bring 'much-needed natural gas supply into the state.'

With Arizona's population and businesses continuing to grow, 'the need to support natural gas power generation is going to continue to expand,' she said.

Nick Myers said that before he became chair of the Arizona Corporation Commission in 2026, the Arizona natural gas industry said the state's pipelines were at capacity.

He added that Arizona's pipelines have been this way for numerous years.

Arizona utilities companies will have enough gas until the pipeline is built, Myers told The Center Square on Wednesday.

To meet the state's increasing demand, Arizona needed another natural gas source, he said.

Arizona has numerous renewable energy sources, but to expand them, the state needed more natural gas, Myers noted.

Natural gas plays a big role in Arizona's economy, Myers said, calling it the 'foundation of (Arizona's) energy structure.' In 2024, natural gas-fired power plants provided 45% of Arizona's electricity generation, according to the U.S. Energy Information Administration.

The Palo Verde Generating Station is the backbone of Arizona's energy grid, while natural gas is 'the tool that allows (Arizona) to expand and grow until the point where nuclear is a viable option,' Myers said.

Myers said most pipeline regulation takes place at the federal level, but the ACC also regulates railroad and pipeline safety in Arizona.

He added that the ACC attempts to ensure Arizona's energy providers are 'maintaining an appropriate balance of gas versus solar, wind and nuclear.'

Regarding economic benefits, Energy Transfer estimates the pipeline will create up to 5,000 construction jobs, with another 75 full-time positions to operate and maintain it, Granado said.

She noted these full-time jobs will be spread across New Mexico and Arizona.

With the new pipeline coming in, the ACC approved converting certain coal plants to natural gas plants, Myers said. Without the pipeline, Myers said, these coal plants would have been shut down in the 'early to mid 2030s.'

According to Myers, keeping these plants open allows energy to remain on the state's electrical grid and keeps 'communities functioning.'

He estimated hundreds of Arizona jobs will be saved as these coal plants switch to natural gas.
